- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
agents
datalake-ci-permissions
README.md
SKILL.md
---
---
name: datalake-ci-permissions
description: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
team: de
---

# Datalake CI Permissions

Keep all edits scoped to datalake unless the user explicitly asks for another environment.

## Work in Scope

- Edit only `gitlab-ci-aws-permissions/environments/datalake/**`.
- Edit only `infrastructure/**/environments/datalake/**` or `infrastructure-platform/**/environments/datalake/**`.
- Reuse existing datalake naming, team, and repository patterns before adding new ones.

Do not change `dev`, `sandbox`, `prod`, `services`, or other non-datalake environments by default.

## Before Editing

1. Identify which component is being added or changed under `infrastructure/` or `infrastructure-platform/`.
2. Read the component README to confirm expected `terraform.tfvars` input names and integration behaviour. Common READMEs:
   - `infrastructure/s3/README.md` for S3 bucket configuration.
   - `infrastructure/sns/README.md` when SNS topics subscribe Lambda functions or other consumers.
   - `infrastructure/sqs/README.md` when SQS queues trigger downstream processing.
   - `infrastructure/kinesis/README.md` when Kinesis streams create event source mappings.
   - `infrastructure/dynamodb/README.md` for DynamoDB table access.
   - `infrastructure/rds/README.md` for RDS instance access.
3. Open `gitlab-ci-aws-permissions/environments/datalake/global/data/terraform.yaml` and find the closest existing role for the same team or repository.

Prefer nearby datalake examples over inventing a new structure.

## Choose the Right Permission Pattern

Scan `gitlab-ci-aws-permissions/README.md` and `gitlab-ci-aws-permissions/terraform/policy-*.tf` for the full list of available templated policies. Use the narrowest one that covers the requested capability. Common patterns:

- `s3-readonly` / `s3-readwrite`: Use for S3 read or read/write access. Scope to specific `s3_bucket_names` where possible.
- `ecr-push`: Use when the project builds and pushes Docker images.
- `cf-deploy`: Use when the project deploys CloudFormation or serverless stacks. Add `services: ["lambda"]` when the stack creates or updates Lambda functions or layers. Add `s3_bucket_names` only when the deployment package bucket is not covered by defaults.
- `lambda-permission-deploy`: Use when deployment needs `lambda:AddPermission` or `lambda:RemovePermission` for invokers such as API Gateway, SNS, EventBridge, ELB, CloudWatch alarms, or cross-account principals.
- `lambda-function-url`: Use when deployment needs Lambda function URL permissions. Set `lambda_prefixes`, `teams`, and `principals` to the narrowest valid values.

Prefer an existing templated policy over adding broad inline statements.

## Edit Workflow

1. Update the datalake component configuration under the relevant `environments/datalake/...` path.
2. Update the matching datalake CI role in `gitlab-ci-aws-permissions/environments/datalake/global/data/terraform.yaml`.
3. For existing roles: extend them when the same repository and trust boundary already exist and only a new permission is needed. Add a new role only when the repository, environment, or branch scope differs from all existing roles.
4. Keep `trusted_projects.paths` restricted to the exact repository or smallest valid wildcard.

input.prompt:
I'm building a Claude skill that helps engineers write/update AWS SAM `template.yaml` files for AWS Lambda projects, in the style used by the `dns-de-automation` repo. Survey the existing SAM templates under `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/*/template.yaml` (about 30 templates) and identify the dominant, repeating patterns I should encode as guidance.

I need a structured report covering, with concrete examples and file paths:

1. **Template skeleton**: AWSTemplateFormatVersion, Transform, Globals (Function defaults — Runtime, Timeout, MemorySize, Tracing, Architectures, Environment vars, etc.). What runtime versions are common.
2. **Parameters**: typical Parameters block — what params every template tends to declare (Env, AccountId, LogLevel, ImageTag, vpc/subnet/securityGroup, etc.) and their AllowedValues / Defaults.
3. **Mappings & Conditions**: are env-specific Mappings used? Common conditions?
4. **Function resource patterns**: PackageType (Image vs Zip), CodeUri/Layers, Handler, Role vs Policies, EventInvokeConfig, ReservedConcurrentExecutions, DeadLetterQueue, VpcConfig, Tagging, naming convention for FunctionName.
5. **IAM**: do they use inline `Policies:` with SAM policy templates, or attach external `Role` ARNs (e.g., from serverless-access-manager)? Show examples of both if present.
6. **Events**: Schedule, SQS, S3, EventBridge, ApiGateway, Kinesis — which are common, how are they wired.
7. **Supporting resources**: SQS queues + DLQs, SNS topics, DynamoDB tables, LogGroups (explicit AWS::Logs::LogGroup with retention), Alarms (CloudWatch), SSM Parameter usage.
8. **Outputs**: typical Outputs — function ARN, name, exports.
9. **Tags**: common tag keys/values (Team, Service, CostCentre, Environment).
10. **Conventions / oddities**: anything that's clearly a house style (naming with `!Sub '${Env}-...'`, Layer ordering, log retention values, parameter store naming).

Pick ~5-6 representative templates from different services (mix of image-based and zip-based, with/without VPC, with various event sources) and quote short snippets so I can encode them as canonical examples. Cite file paths.

Also note: any usage of `serverless-access-manager` (search for `AccessManager`, `access-manager`, `ServerlessAccessManager`, or imports of access manager roles via ImportValue or SSM) — this is important because the skill also needs to integrate with that tool.

Keep the report focused and structured. Don't write code, just report findings.

Read the README and any related top-level docs of `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager` (start with `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager/README.md`, plus `CONTRIBUTING.md`, and any `docs/` folder if it exists). Also peek at `template.yaml` in that repo to understand what resources/exports it provides.

I'm building a Claude skill that helps engineers create or update SAM `template.yaml` files in another repo (`dns-de-automation`). The skill needs to know how to wire those templates up to use `serverless-access-manager` for IAM correctly.

Report a focused summary covering:

1. **What is serverless-access-manager** — what problem it solves, what it produces (IAM roles? policy ARNs? exported via CloudFormation Outputs or SSM parameters?).
2. **How a consumer SAM template integrates** — concrete patterns/snippets from the README showing how a Lambda function adopts a role or policy from access-manager. Quote exact YAML snippets.
3. **Naming conventions** — role names, export names, SSM parameter names. What inputs the consumer must provide (team, service, env, etc.).
4. **Required vs optional fields** — what consumers must specify, what defaults exist.
5. **Permission model / templated policies** — list the named permission patterns/policies available (e.g., s3-read, dynamo-write) and what they grant.
6. **Anti-patterns** — anything the README explicitly warns against (e.g., "do not inline IAM policies in your template"), or rules that must be followed.
7. **How permissions are added** — does the consumer edit a YAML config in the access-manager repo, or declare something in their own template, or both?

Quote sections of the README directly with line citations. If the README is short, paste the most relevant portions verbatim. Keep the report under ~600 words but include actual code/YAML snippets.

Don't write code. Just report.

content.text:
I'll deliver my findings directly as a message, since the user asked for a report (no file edits required, and plan mode forbids edits to other files anyway).

---

# Report: serverless-access-manager (SAM) integration guide

Source: `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager/README.md`, `template.yaml`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

## 1. What it is

A Lambda-backed CloudFormation **custom resource** (`Custom::ServerlessAccessManager`) that abstracts away per-Lambda supporting infrastructure. The deployed function in each account is named `paas-serverless-access-manager`.

README lines 39-46:
> "Deployment of Lambda functions to AWS requires additional supporting resources to function correctly... An IAM Role, used as an execution role... VPC Security Group... Log forwarding to Datadog. The purpose of the serverless-access-manager function is to abstract the creation of these resources..."

What it produces (from `Attributes` table, README lines 192-198) — exposed via `!GetAtt` on the custom resource (NOT via CloudFormation Exports or SSM):

| Attribute | Notes |
|---|---|
| `FunctionName` | Echo of the input `FunctionName` |
| `LambdaExecutionRoleArn` | ARN of provisioned IAM role |
| `LambdaExecutionRoleName` | Friendly name of role |
| `VpcConfig` | Map for VPC-based Lambda config (when VPC needed) |
| `VpcSgId` | Security Group ID (when VPC needed) |

The repo's own `template.yaml` only outputs `ManagerFunctionArn`, `ManagerFunctionIamRoleArn`, `Region` — i.e. operational info about the manager itself, not per-consumer exports.

## 2. Consumer integration pattern

Canonical snippet (README lines 219-251):

```yaml
Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      Sources:
        MyTopic:
          Type: MSK
          Properties:
            Topics:
              - myteam-topic
      Targets:
        MyDynamoTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: myteam-table

  HelloWorldLambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
```

For VPC execution (README lines 286-296), additionally pass:
```yaml
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
```
and set `VpcExecution: true` on the custom resource.

## 3. Naming conventions / inputs

- `ServiceToken` is **always** `arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager` (region pinned to eu-west-1 in all examples).
- `FunctionName` is the only **required** input (README line 143). It is the identity key — both the consumer's `AWS::Serverless::Function` and the custom resource MUST use the same value. Several examples reuse it via `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName`.
- IAM role naming is internal — consumers reference it only via `!GetAtt`. Cross-account Kinesis trust example references it as `arn:aws:iam::<account-id>:role/lambda-execution-roles/<lambda-execution-role>` (README line 522).
- Resource discovery uses tags `created_by` and `cloudformation:stack-id` (README line 118). Stack tags are validated: must be present, all keys/values lowercase (lines 88-92).

## 4. Required vs optional

Required:
- `ServiceToken`, `FunctionName`.

Optional (README lines 145-148):
- `DatadogLogging` (default `"true"`)
- `Sources` (map)
- `Targets` (map)
- `VpcExecution` (default `"false"`; auto-enabled by ElasticSearch/MSK target)

## 5. Permission model — built-in source/target types

Permissions are **not** named policies you pick from a list. They are derived from the `Type` of each entry under `Sources` (read-only) or `Targets` (read-write).

Sources (README lines 154-167): `Connect`, `DynamoDB` (Table or StreamArn), `ElasticSearch` (forces VPC), `Kinesis`, `Lex`, `MSK`, `S3`, `SNS`, `SQS`, `SSM` (path-prefixed: `/corp-sys/`, `/sc-spange/`, `/dns/`, `/datascience/`), `Transcribe`.

Targets (README lines 173-184): `Connect`, `DynamoDB`, `ElasticSearch`, `EventBridge` (`Bus`), `LambdaFunctionUrl` (AWS_IAM only), `MSK` (forces VPC), `S3`, `SNS`, `SQS`, `Transcribe`.

Note SSM source has limited path prefixes; document this in your skill.

## 6. Anti-patterns / rules

- Examples never inline IAM policies on `AWS::Serverless::Function`; the consumer assigns `Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn` and lets the manager own permissions.
- The custom resource and Lambda must agree on `FunctionName` (README lines 116-120: discovery by name+tags).
- Stack tags must be lowercase and required tags present, otherwise validation fails (lines 88-92).
- "These examples do not include the **creation** of the event trigger, this needs to be configured separately" (line 204) — consumers still need to wire `Events:` blocks or `AWS::Lambda::EventSourceMapping` themselves.
- Cross-account Kinesis: `Sources.Kinesis.Stream` alone does **not** trigger the Lambda; permission + event-source-mapping must be added separately, and the role ARN must be added to the Kinesis stream's principal list in the `infrastructure` repo (lines 490-552).
- Transcribe: silent timeouts in private subnets without NAT/PrivateLink (lines 416-420).

## 7. How permissions are added

Permissions are **declared in the consumer's own SAM template** by adding entries to `Sources`/`Targets` on the `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` resource. There is no YAML config in the `serverless-access-manager` repo that consumers edit. The only exception is cross-account Kinesis source policies, which require a separate MR to the `infrastructure` repo (`kinesis/<env>/<region>/<your-stream>/terraform.tfvars`, README lines 508-539).

## Other notes

- `CONTRIBUTING.md` is project-internal (uv, make deps/test/fmt/lint, trunk-based, Conventional Commits) — not relevant to consumer integration.
- No `docs/` folder exists.
- The manager itself runs on Python 3.14, in eu-west-1, with stack-name == function-name.

content.text:
I'll deliver my findings directly as a message, since the user asked for a report (no file edits required, and plan mode forbids edits to other files anyway).

---

# Report: serverless-access-manager (SAM) integration guide

Source: `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager/README.md`, `template.yaml`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

## 1. What it is

A Lambda-backed CloudFormation **custom resource** (`Custom::ServerlessAccessManager`) that abstracts away per-Lambda supporting infrastructure. The deployed function in each account is named `paas-serverless-access-manager`.

README lines 39-46:
> "Deployment of Lambda functions to AWS requires additional supporting resources to function correctly... An IAM Role, used as an execution role... VPC Security Group... Log forwarding to Datadog. The purpose of the serverless-access-manager function is to abstract the creation of these resources..."

What it produces (from `Attributes` table, README lines 192-198) — exposed via `!GetAtt` on the custom resource (NOT via CloudFormation Exports or SSM):

| Attribute | Notes |
|---|---|
| `FunctionName` | Echo of the input `FunctionName` |
| `LambdaExecutionRoleArn` | ARN of provisioned IAM role |
| `LambdaExecutionRoleName` | Friendly name of role |
| `VpcConfig` | Map for VPC-based Lambda config (when VPC needed) |
| `VpcSgId` | Security Group ID (when VPC needed) |

The repo's own `template.yaml` only outputs `ManagerFunctionArn`, `ManagerFunctionIamRoleArn`, `Region` — i.e. operational info about the manager itself, not per-consumer exports.

## 2. Consumer integration pattern

Canonical snippet (README lines 219-251):

```yaml
Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      Sources:
        MyTopic:
          Type: MSK
          Properties:
            Topics:
              - myteam-topic
      Targets:
        MyDynamoTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: myteam-table

  HelloWorldLambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
```

For VPC execution (README lines 286-296), additionally pass:
```yaml
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
```
and set `VpcExecution: true` on the custom resource.

## 3. Naming conventions / inputs

- `ServiceToken` is **always** `arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager` (region pinned to eu-west-1 in all examples).
- `FunctionName` is the only **required** input (README line 143). It is the identity key — both the consumer's `AWS::Serverless::Function` and the custom resource MUST use the same value. Several examples reuse it via `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName`.
- IAM role naming is internal — consumers reference it only via `!GetAtt`. Cross-account Kinesis trust example references it as `arn:aws:iam::<account-id>:role/lambda-execution-roles/<lambda-execution-role>` (README line 522).
- Resource discovery uses tags `created_by` and `cloudformation:stack-id` (README line 118). Stack tags are validated: must be present, all keys/values lowercase (lines 88-92).

## 4. Required vs optional

Required:
- `ServiceToken`, `FunctionName`.

Optional (README lines 145-148):
- `DatadogLogging` (default `"true"`)
- `Sources` (map)
- `Targets` (map)
- `VpcExecution` (default `"false"`; auto-enabled by ElasticSearch/MSK target)

## 5. Permission model — built-in source/target types

Permissions are **not** named policies you pick from a list. They are derived from the `Type` of each entry under `Sources` (read-only) or `Targets` (read-write).

Sources (README lines 154-167): `Connect`, `DynamoDB` (Table or StreamArn), `ElasticSearch` (forces VPC), `Kinesis`, `Lex`, `MSK`, `S3`, `SNS`, `SQS`, `SSM` (path-prefixed: `/corp-sys/`, `/sc-spange/`, `/dns/`, `/datascience/`), `Transcribe`.

Targets (README lines 173-184): `Connect`, `DynamoDB`, `ElasticSearch`, `EventBridge` (`Bus`), `LambdaFunctionUrl` (AWS_IAM only), `MSK` (forces VPC), `S3`, `SNS`, `SQS`, `Transcribe`.

Note SSM source has limited path prefixes; document this in your skill.

## 6. Anti-patterns / rules

- Examples never inline IAM policies on `AWS::Serverless::Function`; the consumer assigns `Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn` and lets the manager own permissions.
- The custom resource and Lambda must agree on `FunctionName` (README lines 116-120: discovery by name+tags).
- Stack tags must be lowercase and required tags present, otherwise validation fails (lines 88-92).
- "These examples do not include the **creation** of the event trigger, this needs to be configured separately" (line 204) — consumers still need to wire `Events:` blocks or `AWS::Lambda::EventSourceMapping` themselves.
- Cross-account Kinesis: `Sources.Kinesis.Stream` alone does **not** trigger the Lambda; permission + event-source-mapping must be added separately, and the role ARN must be added to the Kinesis stream's principal list in the `infrastructure` repo (lines 490-552).
- Transcribe: silent timeouts in private subnets without NAT/PrivateLink (lines 416-420).

## 7. How permissions are added

Permissions are **declared in the consumer's own SAM template** by adding entries to `Sources`/`Targets` on the `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` resource. There is no YAML config in the `serverless-access-manager` repo that consumers edit. The only exception is cross-account Kinesis source policies, which require a separate MR to the `infrastructure` repo (`kinesis/<env>/<region>/<your-stream>/terraform.tfvars`, README lines 508-539).

## Other notes

- `CONTRIBUTING.md` is project-internal (uv, make deps/test/fmt/lint, trunk-based, Conventional Commits) — not relevant to consumer integration.
- No `docs/` folder exists.
- The manager itself runs on Python 3.14, in eu-west-1, with stack-name == function-name.

content.text:
I have comprehensive coverage. All 32 templates use `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager`. Here is the structured report.

---

# Survey of SAM templates in `dns-de-automation/lambda/*`

Surveyed all 32 `template.yaml` files. The dominant pattern across the entire repo is: **a SAM Serverless Function whose IAM role and VPC config come from a `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` resource**. This drives everything else.

## 1. Template skeleton

Every template follows an identical 3-line header:

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: <one-line service description>
```

- **No `Globals:` block** is used in any template. Function defaults (Runtime, Timeout, MemorySize, Tracing, Architectures, Environment) are repeated per-function. This is a clear gap the skill could codify.
- **Architectures**: explicit only in `realtime-product-recommendations-api` (`Architectures: [x86_64]`). Otherwise unset (defaults).
- **Tracing**: never set.
- **Common Runtime versions** (across 32 templates): `python3.13` (most recent additions, ~10 templates), `python3.11`, `python3.12`, `python3.8` (legacy), `python3.9` (one). New code should default to `python3.13`.
- **Common MemorySize**: `2048` (default workhorse, ~14 templates), `1024`, `1500`, `256`, `1769` (only in realtime-recs API — "minimum memory for which we still get 1 full vCPU"), `10240` (image-based heavy ingestion). Frequent comment-driven choices.
- **Common Timeout**: `900` (vast majority — long-running ingest), `300` (lighter), `60`/`30` (API/notification).
- **EphemeralStorage `Size: 10240`**: used when temp file space matters (file-ingestion-framework, ingest-api, event-triggered-ingest, rte-reporting).

## 2. Parameters

Every template has a `Parameters:` block. The recurring parameters:

- `Environment` — `Type: String`. Universal. Always referenced as `${Environment}` in the function name. **No `AllowedValues` / `Default` is ever set.**
- `TeamName` — `Type: String, Default: de` (sometimes `DE`). Used for tag values.
- `ApplicationName` — `Type: String, Default: '<app-name>'`. Used for tag values.
- `Region` — `Type: String`. Used to set the `Region` env var (not for resource placement).
- `EventBus` — `Type: String`. Name of the Supernova EventBridge bus.
- `LogLevel` — `Type: String, Default: 'INFO'`. Description: `Log level can be DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR or CRITICAL`. **Never uses `AllowedValues`.**
- `ImageTag` — `Type: String, Description: "Image SHA"`. Only for image-based functions.
- `LambdaRole` — `Type: String, Description: ARN of Lambda IAM Role` (used when the role is provisioned outside SAM).
- `CrossAccountRole` — `Type: String`. STS role to assume.
- `Channel` / `DeChannel` / `DsChannel` — Slack alert channel.
- `<X>LayerVersion` — e.g. `SupernovaLayerVersion`, `MetricsPublisherLayerVersion`, `DeCommonLayer` — passed in as ARNs from the calling pipeline.
- Various `<X>SSM` / `SSM<X>` / `<X>Secret` parameters carrying SSM parameter names or Secrets Manager ARNs (e.g. `ClientSecretSSM`, `SSMOpenAIAdminKey`, `SSMMergedTableName`, `SupermetricsSecret`).

**No template declares VPC subnets / security groups directly as parameters** — that responsibility is delegated to `ServerlessAccessManager` via `VpcExecution: 'true'`. There is also no `AccountId` parameter; templates use `${AWS::AccountId}` pseudo-param directly.

## 3. Mappings & Conditions

- **`Mappings:` — never used.**
- **`Conditions:` — used in exactly one template**: `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/zendesk-alerter/template.yaml` (top-level `Conditions:` keyword found, but functionally light). Env-specific behaviour is generally encoded as parallel `Dev*` / `Prod*` named `Targets` inside `ServerlessAccessManager` rather than via Conditions/Mappings — see `request-batcher`, `validator`, `rte-batcher-guard-checks`, etc.

## 4. Function resource patterns

- **PackageType**: 
  - **Zip (default, ~30 templates)**: `CodeUri: src/`, `Handler: app.lambda_handler` (or `<module>.lambda_handler`), `Runtime: pythonX.Y`.
  - **Image (2 templates)**: `file-ingestion-framework`, `realtime-product-recommendations-api`. Pattern:
    ```yaml
    PackageType: Image
    ImageUri: !Sub "334512769709.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/<repo>/<service>/${Environment}:${ImageTag}"
    ```
    The ECR account `334512769709` is hard-coded and the prefix is either `dns-de` or `data-de`.
- **FunctionName**: almost always `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName` (so it's defined once on the access manager, reused on the function). Three exceptions hard-code `!Sub '<prefix>-${Environment}'` directly on the function (`braze-batcher`, `event-triggered-ingest`, `p13n-dynamo-db`).
- **Naming convention**: `<prefix>-de-<service>-${Environment}` where prefix is `dns` (older) or `data` (newer). Examples: `dns-de-file-ingestion-framework-${Environment}`, `data-de-validator-${Environment}`, `data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-${Environment}`.
- **Role**: 
  - `Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn` — the dominant pattern (~28 templates).
  - `Role: !Ref LambdaRole` — used when the role is provisioned upstream and passed as a CFN parameter (`braze-batcher`, `optimisa-email-service`, `p13n-dynamo-db`).
  - `Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/realtime-recommendations-scale-to-zero"` — directly constructed ARN (only `realtime-product-recommendations-api`).
- **`Policies:` (SAM policy templates)**: never used. Inline IAM is added via separate `AWS::IAM::Policy` / `AWS::IAM::RolePolicy` resources attached to the access-manager's role (see §5).
- **VpcConfig**: `VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig` whenever VPC is needed (controlled by `VpcExecution: 'true'` on the access manager). Functions using `Role: !Ref LambdaRole` typically omit VpcConfig.
- **Layers**: when used, references like `Layers: - !Ref SupernovaLayerVersion` and/or `- !Ref DeCommonLayer` and/or `- !Ref MetricsPublisherLayerVersion`. Order: Supernova layer typically first, common layer next. One legacy template (`metapack-pts-ingest-sftp`) defines its own `AWS::Serverless::LayerVersion` for `awswrangler-layer-2.7.0`.
- **EventInvokeConfig / DeadLetterQueue / ReservedConcurrentExecutions**: not used in any template.
- **EphemeralStorage**: `Size: 10240` when needed (file-ingestion-framework, ingest-api, event-triggered-ingest, rte-reporting).

## 5. IAM patterns

The dominant style is: **let `ServerlessAccessManager` create the execution role, then attach extra inline policies to that role** when SAM-style resource targets are not enough.

Two equivalent attachment styles appear:

- `AWS::IAM::Policy` with `Roles: [ !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName ]`. Examples: `ingest-api/template.yaml` (lines 59-81), `personal-data-catalog/template.yaml` (lines 46-59), `zendesk-alerter/template.yaml` (lines 36-50).
- `AWS::IAM::RolePolicy` with `RoleName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName`. Examples: `clean-rooms-query-trigger/template.yaml` (lines 18-32), `aws-resource-explorer-exporter/template.yaml` (lines 46-57).

The ~28 templates that hand off the role to `ServerlessAccessManager` rely on the access manager's `Targets`/`Sources` to grant S3, DynamoDB, EventBridge, SQS, SSM, MSK access. This is the canonical mechanism — **inline `Policies:` with SAM policy templates (`S3CrudPolicy`, `DynamoDBReadPolicy`, etc.) are not used anywhere**.

A handful of templates (`braze-batcher`, `optimisa-email-service`, `p13n-dynamo-db`) skip the access-manager-as-role and accept a `LambdaRole` parameter (provisioned upstream, e.g., from a separate stack or serverless-access-manager invocation in the calling pipeline). The access manager is still declared but only to register `Targets`/`Sources`.

## 6. Events

Almost all event wiring is done **outside** the SAM `Events:` block, via the `ServerlessAccessManager.Targets`/`Sources` and supporting `AWS::Lambda::Permission` resources.

- **SAM `Events:` block usage**: only in `zendesk-alerter/template.yaml`:
  ```yaml
  Events:
    SNSTopicTrigger:
      Type: SNS
      Properties:
        Topic: !Ref SNSTopicARN
  ```
- **EventBridge rule -> Lambda**: declared as `AWS::Lambda::Permission` granting `events.amazonaws.com` invoke rights. Examples: `file-ingestion-framework/template.yaml` lines 146-152, `ingest-complete-event-router/template.yaml` lines 70-76.
- **S3 -> Lambda**: per-bucket `AWS::Lambda::Permission` resource. Example: `event-triggered-ingest/template.yaml` lines 98-114 (one per `hbi-dns-landing` and `hbi-dns-dev-landing`).
- **MSK / Kafka source**: declared in the access manager's `Sources:` block as `Type: MSK`. Example: `kafka-dynamodb-integrator/template.yaml` lines 35-40.
- **ALB / API Gateway**: only `realtime-product-recommendations-api` uses ALB. No API Gateway / Schedule events anywhere.
- **SQS, EventBridge, DynamoDB, S3 targets**: registered as named `Targets` under the access manager. Common Targets pattern: parallel `Dev*`/`Prod*` entries pointing at env-named buckets/tables/queues so the same template works in both environments. Example: `validator/template.yaml` lines 22-80.

## 7. Supporting resources

The templates are deliberately thin: **no template defines its own `AWS::SQS::Queue`, `AWS::SNS::Topic`, `AWS::DynamoDB::Table`, `AWS::Logs::LogGroup`, `AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm`** — except:

- `realtime-product-recommendations-api/template.yaml` has rich supporting resources:
  - `AWS::Lambda::Version`, `AWS::Lambda::Alias` (with `ProvisionedConcurrencyConfig`).
  - `AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalableTarget` + `ScalingPolicy` for provisioned concurrency.
  - `AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm` (`PcNoDataAlarm`) wired to a second Lambda (`ScaleToMinFunction`, defined inline with `InlineCode:`).
  - `AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup` and `ListenerRule`.
- `metapack-pts-ingest-sftp` and `single-unit-adjustments` define an `AWS::Serverless::LayerVersion` (`AWSDataWranglerLayer`).
- LogGroups with explicit retention: not configured in any template (they rely on the lambda-default LogGroup).
- SSM parameter consumption is via `!Sub '{{resolve:ssm:${ParamName}}}'` in env vars, e.g. `request-batcher/template.yaml` line 96, `braze-segment-exporter/template.yaml` line 59. SSM access is granted by listing the parameter names under `ServerlessAccessManager.Sources.<name>.Type: SSM` (with optional wildcard like `/dns/sftp/*`).

## 8. Outputs

Every template defines exactly one Output: the function ARN.

```yaml
Outputs:
  <FunctionLogicalId>FunctionARN:
    Description: "<service> function ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt <FunctionLogicalId>.Arn
```

No `Export:` blocks, no cross-stack references via Outputs.

## 9. Tags

Tagging is inconsistently applied:
- About half the templates set `Tags:` on the function. About half omit it entirely.
- Common tag keys when present:
  - `application: '<service-name>'` or `!Ref ApplicationName`
  - `created_by: cloudformation`
  - `owner: 'de'`
  - `team: !Ref TeamName` (sometimes literal `'DE'` / `'de'`)
  - `project: '<project-name>'` (less common)
  - `product: '<product-name>'` (less common)
- **Not used anywhere**: `CostCentre`, `Environment` (as a tag), `Service`. Naming style is lowercase keys with hyphens stripped from values. A canonical example is `data-catalog-copier/template.yaml` lines 49-55.

## 10. Conventions / oddities

- **House-style FunctionName**: `!Sub '<prefix>-de-<service>-${Environment}'`. The two prefixes are `dns-de-` (older modules) and `data-de-` (newer). The skill should pick one consistently and probably ask the user.
- **Logical IDs use PascalCase + `Function` suffix** (`ValidatorFunction`, `BrazeBatcherLambdaFunction`, `RteReportingFunction`).
- **`Region` parameter is informational only** (passed as env var) — it's never used to drive resource placement. The hard-coded region is `eu-west-1`.
- **Hard-coded AWS account IDs** appear in literal ARNs: `334512769709` (this is the deploy account; appears in 6+ templates as part of ECR or EventBridge rule ARNs).
- **`paas-serverless-access-manager` is universally available**: every template references `arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager` as the `ServiceToken` — this is provisioned as a custom-resource Lambda in the deploy account. No template imports it via `ImportValue` or SSM; the assumed convention is "it just exists."
- **Env vars use mixed casing** (`Env`, `ENV`, `Environment` all appear); `Region` and `BusName` show up frequently.
- **Comments commonly survive in templates** (commented-out resources/blocks): `realtime-product-recommendations-api`, `zendesk-alerter`, `single-unit-adjustments`, `p13n-dynamo-db`. The skill should probably not preserve these.
- **Two GitLab/CI pipeline-injected parameters** show up by convention but never as constants: `ImageTag` (the deploy SHA), `<X>LayerVersion` (Lambda layer ARNs).

## ServerlessAccessManager findings (critical for the skill)

**32 of 32 templates use `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager`.** It is the central abstraction the skill must integrate with. Search hits for `AccessManager`/`access-manager`/`ServerlessAccessManager`:

- ServiceToken (universal): `!Sub 'arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager'`
- It exposes these `!GetAtt` outputs that templates consume:
  - `FunctionName` — used as `Properties.FunctionName` on `AWS::Serverless::Function`
  - `LambdaExecutionRoleArn` — used as `Properties.Role`
  - `LambdaExecutionRoleName` — used as `RoleName` for `AWS::IAM::Policy`/`RolePolicy` to attach extra perms
  - `VpcConfig` — used as `Properties.VpcConfig` (subnets + security groups)
- Properties commonly set:
  - `FunctionName: !Sub '<prefix>-de-<service>-${Environment}'`
  - `VpcExecution: 'true'` (almost universal) or `'false'` (rare; e.g. `p13n-dynamo-db`)
  - `DatadogLogging: 'false'` (only `zendesk-alerter` sets this; default appears to be true)
  - `Targets:` map of named entries — supported `Type` values seen: `S3`, `DynamoDB`, `EventBridge`, `SQS`. Properties depend on type: `Bucket`, `Table` (supports `<table>/index/<gsi>` form for GSI grants — see `request-batcher`), `Bus`, `Queue`.
  - `Sources:` map of named entries — supported `Type` values seen: `S3`, `SSM` (with `Properties.Parameters: [list]`), `MSK` (with `Properties.Topics: [list]`).
- **No template uses `ImportValue` or SSM lookup to pull access-manager outputs into another stack**; consumption is always via `!GetAtt` on the local `ServerlessAccessManager` resource.

## Recommended representative examples for the skill

Use these as canonical templates to point users at:

1. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/openai-cost-exporter/template.yaml` — minimal-but-complete zip + EventBridge target + SSM source + tags. Excellent default skeleton.
2. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/file-ingestion-framework/template.yaml` — image-based + many S3 targets + SSM sources + ECR ImageUri convention + Lambda::Permission for an EventBridge rule.
3. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/ingest-api/template.yaml` — zip + Layer + extra inline `AWS::IAM::Policy` attached to access-manager's role.
4. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/event-triggered-ingest/template.yaml` — S3 trigger via `AWS::Lambda::Permission` + Layer + EphemeralStorage.
5. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/kafka-dynamodb-integrator/template.yaml` — MSK source + DynamoDB target.
6. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/realtime-product-recommendations-api/template.yaml` — image-based ALB-fronted with Version/Alias/ProvisionedConcurrency + Application Auto Scaling + CloudWatch alarm. Useful as a reference for "complex" cases but explicitly an outlier.
7. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/zendesk-alerter/template.yaml` — only template using the SAM-native `Events:` block (SNS) and the only one setting `DatadogLogging: 'false'`.

toolUseResult.prompt:
I'm building a Claude skill that helps engineers write/update AWS SAM `template.yaml` files for AWS Lambda projects, in the style used by the `dns-de-automation` repo. Survey the existing SAM templates under `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/*/template.yaml` (about 30 templates) and identify the dominant, repeating patterns I should encode as guidance.

I need a structured report covering, with concrete examples and file paths:

1. **Template skeleton**: AWSTemplateFormatVersion, Transform, Globals (Function defaults — Runtime, Timeout, MemorySize, Tracing, Architectures, Environment vars, etc.). What runtime versions are common.
2. **Parameters**: typical Parameters block — what params every template tends to declare (Env, AccountId, LogLevel, ImageTag, vpc/subnet/securityGroup, etc.) and their AllowedValues / Defaults.
3. **Mappings & Conditions**: are env-specific Mappings used? Common conditions?
4. **Function resource patterns**: PackageType (Image vs Zip), CodeUri/Layers, Handler, Role vs Policies, EventInvokeConfig, ReservedConcurrentExecutions, DeadLetterQueue, VpcConfig, Tagging, naming convention for FunctionName.
5. **IAM**: do they use inline `Policies:` with SAM policy templates, or attach external `Role` ARNs (e.g., from serverless-access-manager)? Show examples of both if present.
6. **Events**: Schedule, SQS, S3, EventBridge, ApiGateway, Kinesis — which are common, how are they wired.
7. **Supporting resources**: SQS queues + DLQs, SNS topics, DynamoDB tables, LogGroups (explicit AWS::Logs::LogGroup with retention), Alarms (CloudWatch), SSM Parameter usage.
8. **Outputs**: typical Outputs — function ARN, name, exports.
9. **Tags**: common tag keys/values (Team, Service, CostCentre, Environment).
10. **Conventions / oddities**: anything that's clearly a house style (naming with `!Sub '${Env}-...'`, Layer ordering, log retention values, parameter store naming).

Pick ~5-6 representative templates from different services (mix of image-based and zip-based, with/without VPC, with various event sources) and quote short snippets so I can encode them as canonical examples. Cite file paths.

Also note: any usage of `serverless-access-manager` (search for `AccessManager`, `access-manager`, `ServerlessAccessManager`, or imports of access manager roles via ImportValue or SSM) — this is important because the skill also needs to integrate with that tool.

Keep the report focused and structured. Don't write code, just report findings.

content.text:
I have comprehensive coverage. All 32 templates use `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager`. Here is the structured report.

---

# Survey of SAM templates in `dns-de-automation/lambda/*`

Surveyed all 32 `template.yaml` files. The dominant pattern across the entire repo is: **a SAM Serverless Function whose IAM role and VPC config come from a `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` resource**. This drives everything else.

## 1. Template skeleton

Every template follows an identical 3-line header:

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: <one-line service description>
```

- **No `Globals:` block** is used in any template. Function defaults (Runtime, Timeout, MemorySize, Tracing, Architectures, Environment) are repeated per-function. This is a clear gap the skill could codify.
- **Architectures**: explicit only in `realtime-product-recommendations-api` (`Architectures: [x86_64]`). Otherwise unset (defaults).
- **Tracing**: never set.
- **Common Runtime versions** (across 32 templates): `python3.13` (most recent additions, ~10 templates), `python3.11`, `python3.12`, `python3.8` (legacy), `python3.9` (one). New code should default to `python3.13`.
- **Common MemorySize**: `2048` (default workhorse, ~14 templates), `1024`, `1500`, `256`, `1769` (only in realtime-recs API — "minimum memory for which we still get 1 full vCPU"), `10240` (image-based heavy ingestion). Frequent comment-driven choices.
- **Common Timeout**: `900` (vast majority — long-running ingest), `300` (lighter), `60`/`30` (API/notification).
- **EphemeralStorage `Size: 10240`**: used when temp file space matters (file-ingestion-framework, ingest-api, event-triggered-ingest, rte-reporting).

## 2. Parameters

Every template has a `Parameters:` block. The recurring parameters:

- `Environment` — `Type: String`. Universal. Always referenced as `${Environment}` in the function name. **No `AllowedValues` / `Default` is ever set.**
- `TeamName` — `Type: String, Default: de` (sometimes `DE`). Used for tag values.
- `ApplicationName` — `Type: String, Default: '<app-name>'`. Used for tag values.
- `Region` — `Type: String`. Used to set the `Region` env var (not for resource placement).
- `EventBus` — `Type: String`. Name of the Supernova EventBridge bus.
- `LogLevel` — `Type: String, Default: 'INFO'`. Description: `Log level can be DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR or CRITICAL`. **Never uses `AllowedValues`.**
- `ImageTag` — `Type: String, Description: "Image SHA"`. Only for image-based functions.
- `LambdaRole` — `Type: String, Description: ARN of Lambda IAM Role` (used when the role is provisioned outside SAM).
- `CrossAccountRole` — `Type: String`. STS role to assume.
- `Channel` / `DeChannel` / `DsChannel` — Slack alert channel.
- `<X>LayerVersion` — e.g. `SupernovaLayerVersion`, `MetricsPublisherLayerVersion`, `DeCommonLayer` — passed in as ARNs from the calling pipeline.
- Various `<X>SSM` / `SSM<X>` / `<X>Secret` parameters carrying SSM parameter names or Secrets Manager ARNs (e.g. `ClientSecretSSM`, `SSMOpenAIAdminKey`, `SSMMergedTableName`, `SupermetricsSecret`).

**No template declares VPC subnets / security groups directly as parameters** — that responsibility is delegated to `ServerlessAccessManager` via `VpcExecution: 'true'`. There is also no `AccountId` parameter; templates use `${AWS::AccountId}` pseudo-param directly.

## 3. Mappings & Conditions

- **`Mappings:` — never used.**
- **`Conditions:` — used in exactly one template**: `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/zendesk-alerter/template.yaml` (top-level `Conditions:` keyword found, but functionally light). Env-specific behaviour is generally encoded as parallel `Dev*` / `Prod*` named `Targets` inside `ServerlessAccessManager` rather than via Conditions/Mappings — see `request-batcher`, `validator`, `rte-batcher-guard-checks`, etc.

## 4. Function resource patterns

- **PackageType**: 
  - **Zip (default, ~30 templates)**: `CodeUri: src/`, `Handler: app.lambda_handler` (or `<module>.lambda_handler`), `Runtime: pythonX.Y`.
  - **Image (2 templates)**: `file-ingestion-framework`, `realtime-product-recommendations-api`. Pattern:
    ```yaml
    PackageType: Image
    ImageUri: !Sub "334512769709.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/<repo>/<service>/${Environment}:${ImageTag}"
    ```
    The ECR account `334512769709` is hard-coded and the prefix is either `dns-de` or `data-de`.
- **FunctionName**: almost always `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName` (so it's defined once on the access manager, reused on the function). Three exceptions hard-code `!Sub '<prefix>-${Environment}'` directly on the function (`braze-batcher`, `event-triggered-ingest`, `p13n-dynamo-db`).
- **Naming convention**: `<prefix>-de-<service>-${Environment}` where prefix is `dns` (older) or `data` (newer). Examples: `dns-de-file-ingestion-framework-${Environment}`, `data-de-validator-${Environment}`, `data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-${Environment}`.
- **Role**: 
  - `Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn` — the dominant pattern (~28 templates).
  - `Role: !Ref LambdaRole` — used when the role is provisioned upstream and passed as a CFN parameter (`braze-batcher`, `optimisa-email-service`, `p13n-dynamo-db`).
  - `Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/realtime-recommendations-scale-to-zero"` — directly constructed ARN (only `realtime-product-recommendations-api`).
- **`Policies:` (SAM policy templates)**: never used. Inline IAM is added via separate `AWS::IAM::Policy` / `AWS::IAM::RolePolicy` resources attached to the access-manager's role (see §5).
- **VpcConfig**: `VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig` whenever VPC is needed (controlled by `VpcExecution: 'true'` on the access manager). Functions using `Role: !Ref LambdaRole` typically omit VpcConfig.
- **Layers**: when used, references like `Layers: - !Ref SupernovaLayerVersion` and/or `- !Ref DeCommonLayer` and/or `- !Ref MetricsPublisherLayerVersion`. Order: Supernova layer typically first, common layer next. One legacy template (`metapack-pts-ingest-sftp`) defines its own `AWS::Serverless::LayerVersion` for `awswrangler-layer-2.7.0`.
- **EventInvokeConfig / DeadLetterQueue / ReservedConcurrentExecutions**: not used in any template.
- **EphemeralStorage**: `Size: 10240` when needed (file-ingestion-framework, ingest-api, event-triggered-ingest, rte-reporting).

## 5. IAM patterns

The dominant style is: **let `ServerlessAccessManager` create the execution role, then attach extra inline policies to that role** when SAM-style resource targets are not enough.

Two equivalent attachment styles appear:

- `AWS::IAM::Policy` with `Roles: [ !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName ]`. Examples: `ingest-api/template.yaml` (lines 59-81), `personal-data-catalog/template.yaml` (lines 46-59), `zendesk-alerter/template.yaml` (lines 36-50).
- `AWS::IAM::RolePolicy` with `RoleName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName`. Examples: `clean-rooms-query-trigger/template.yaml` (lines 18-32), `aws-resource-explorer-exporter/template.yaml` (lines 46-57).

The ~28 templates that hand off the role to `ServerlessAccessManager` rely on the access manager's `Targets`/`Sources` to grant S3, DynamoDB, EventBridge, SQS, SSM, MSK access. This is the canonical mechanism — **inline `Policies:` with SAM policy templates (`S3CrudPolicy`, `DynamoDBReadPolicy`, etc.) are not used anywhere**.

A handful of templates (`braze-batcher`, `optimisa-email-service`, `p13n-dynamo-db`) skip the access-manager-as-role and accept a `LambdaRole` parameter (provisioned upstream, e.g., from a separate stack or serverless-access-manager invocation in the calling pipeline). The access manager is still declared but only to register `Targets`/`Sources`.

## 6. Events

Almost all event wiring is done **outside** the SAM `Events:` block, via the `ServerlessAccessManager.Targets`/`Sources` and supporting `AWS::Lambda::Permission` resources.

- **SAM `Events:` block usage**: only in `zendesk-alerter/template.yaml`:
  ```yaml
  Events:
    SNSTopicTrigger:
      Type: SNS
      Properties:
        Topic: !Ref SNSTopicARN
  ```
- **EventBridge rule -> Lambda**: declared as `AWS::Lambda::Permission` granting `events.amazonaws.com` invoke rights. Examples: `file-ingestion-framework/template.yaml` lines 146-152, `ingest-complete-event-router/template.yaml` lines 70-76.
- **S3 -> Lambda**: per-bucket `AWS::Lambda::Permission` resource. Example: `event-triggered-ingest/template.yaml` lines 98-114 (one per `hbi-dns-landing` and `hbi-dns-dev-landing`).
- **MSK / Kafka source**: declared in the access manager's `Sources:` block as `Type: MSK`. Example: `kafka-dynamodb-integrator/template.yaml` lines 35-40.
- **ALB / API Gateway**: only `realtime-product-recommendations-api` uses ALB. No API Gateway / Schedule events anywhere.
- **SQS, EventBridge, DynamoDB, S3 targets**: registered as named `Targets` under the access manager. Common Targets pattern: parallel `Dev*`/`Prod*` entries pointing at env-named buckets/tables/queues so the same template works in both environments. Example: `validator/template.yaml` lines 22-80.

## 7. Supporting resources

The templates are deliberately thin: **no template defines its own `AWS::SQS::Queue`, `AWS::SNS::Topic`, `AWS::DynamoDB::Table`, `AWS::Logs::LogGroup`, `AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm`** — except:

- `realtime-product-recommendations-api/template.yaml` has rich supporting resources:
  - `AWS::Lambda::Version`, `AWS::Lambda::Alias` (with `ProvisionedConcurrencyConfig`).
  - `AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalableTarget` + `ScalingPolicy` for provisioned concurrency.
  - `AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm` (`PcNoDataAlarm`) wired to a second Lambda (`ScaleToMinFunction`, defined inline with `InlineCode:`).
  - `AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup` and `ListenerRule`.
- `metapack-pts-ingest-sftp` and `single-unit-adjustments` define an `AWS::Serverless::LayerVersion` (`AWSDataWranglerLayer`).
- LogGroups with explicit retention: not configured in any template (they rely on the lambda-default LogGroup).
- SSM parameter consumption is via `!Sub '{{resolve:ssm:${ParamName}}}'` in env vars, e.g. `request-batcher/template.yaml` line 96, `braze-segment-exporter/template.yaml` line 59. SSM access is granted by listing the parameter names under `ServerlessAccessManager.Sources.<name>.Type: SSM` (with optional wildcard like `/dns/sftp/*`).

## 8. Outputs

Every template defines exactly one Output: the function ARN.

```yaml
Outputs:
  <FunctionLogicalId>FunctionARN:
    Description: "<service> function ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt <FunctionLogicalId>.Arn
```

No `Export:` blocks, no cross-stack references via Outputs.

## 9. Tags

Tagging is inconsistently applied:
- About half the templates set `Tags:` on the function. About half omit it entirely.
- Common tag keys when present:
  - `application: '<service-name>'` or `!Ref ApplicationName`
  - `created_by: cloudformation`
  - `owner: 'de'`
  - `team: !Ref TeamName` (sometimes literal `'DE'` / `'de'`)
  - `project: '<project-name>'` (less common)
  - `product: '<product-name>'` (less common)
- **Not used anywhere**: `CostCentre`, `Environment` (as a tag), `Service`. Naming style is lowercase keys with hyphens stripped from values. A canonical example is `data-catalog-copier/template.yaml` lines 49-55.

## 10. Conventions / oddities

- **House-style FunctionName**: `!Sub '<prefix>-de-<service>-${Environment}'`. The two prefixes are `dns-de-` (older modules) and `data-de-` (newer). The skill should pick one consistently and probably ask the user.
- **Logical IDs use PascalCase + `Function` suffix** (`ValidatorFunction`, `BrazeBatcherLambdaFunction`, `RteReportingFunction`).
- **`Region` parameter is informational only** (passed as env var) — it's never used to drive resource placement. The hard-coded region is `eu-west-1`.
- **Hard-coded AWS account IDs** appear in literal ARNs: `334512769709` (this is the deploy account; appears in 6+ templates as part of ECR or EventBridge rule ARNs).
- **`paas-serverless-access-manager` is universally available**: every template references `arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager` as the `ServiceToken` — this is provisioned as a custom-resource Lambda in the deploy account. No template imports it via `ImportValue` or SSM; the assumed convention is "it just exists."
- **Env vars use mixed casing** (`Env`, `ENV`, `Environment` all appear); `Region` and `BusName` show up frequently.
- **Comments commonly survive in templates** (commented-out resources/blocks): `realtime-product-recommendations-api`, `zendesk-alerter`, `single-unit-adjustments`, `p13n-dynamo-db`. The skill should probably not preserve these.
- **Two GitLab/CI pipeline-injected parameters** show up by convention but never as constants: `ImageTag` (the deploy SHA), `<X>LayerVersion` (Lambda layer ARNs).

## ServerlessAccessManager findings (critical for the skill)

**32 of 32 templates use `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager`.** It is the central abstraction the skill must integrate with. Search hits for `AccessManager`/`access-manager`/`ServerlessAccessManager`:

- ServiceToken (universal): `!Sub 'arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager'`
- It exposes these `!GetAtt` outputs that templates consume:
  - `FunctionName` — used as `Properties.FunctionName` on `AWS::Serverless::Function`
  - `LambdaExecutionRoleArn` — used as `Properties.Role`
  - `LambdaExecutionRoleName` — used as `RoleName` for `AWS::IAM::Policy`/`RolePolicy` to attach extra perms
  - `VpcConfig` — used as `Properties.VpcConfig` (subnets + security groups)
- Properties commonly set:
  - `FunctionName: !Sub '<prefix>-de-<service>-${Environment}'`
  - `VpcExecution: 'true'` (almost universal) or `'false'` (rare; e.g. `p13n-dynamo-db`)
  - `DatadogLogging: 'false'` (only `zendesk-alerter` sets this; default appears to be true)
  - `Targets:` map of named entries — supported `Type` values seen: `S3`, `DynamoDB`, `EventBridge`, `SQS`. Properties depend on type: `Bucket`, `Table` (supports `<table>/index/<gsi>` form for GSI grants — see `request-batcher`), `Bus`, `Queue`.
  - `Sources:` map of named entries — supported `Type` values seen: `S3`, `SSM` (with `Properties.Parameters: [list]`), `MSK` (with `Properties.Topics: [list]`).
- **No template uses `ImportValue` or SSM lookup to pull access-manager outputs into another stack**; consumption is always via `!GetAtt` on the local `ServerlessAccessManager` resource.

## Recommended representative examples for the skill

Use these as canonical templates to point users at:

1. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/openai-cost-exporter/template.yaml` — minimal-but-complete zip + EventBridge target + SSM source + tags. Excellent default skeleton.
2. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/file-ingestion-framework/template.yaml` — image-based + many S3 targets + SSM sources + ECR ImageUri convention + Lambda::Permission for an EventBridge rule.
3. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/ingest-api/template.yaml` — zip + Layer + extra inline `AWS::IAM::Policy` attached to access-manager's role.
4. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/event-triggered-ingest/template.yaml` — S3 trigger via `AWS::Lambda::Permission` + Layer + EphemeralStorage.
5. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/kafka-dynamodb-integrator/template.yaml` — MSK source + DynamoDB target.
6. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/realtime-product-recommendations-api/template.yaml` — image-based ALB-fronted with Version/Alias/ProvisionedConcurrency + Application Auto Scaling + CloudWatch alarm. Useful as a reference for "complex" cases but explicitly an outlier.
7. `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda/zendesk-alerter/template.yaml` — only template using the SAM-native `Events:` block (SNS) and the only one setting `DatadogLogging: 'false'`.

input.content:
# Plan: `sam-template` skill for SAM `template.yaml` authoring

## Context

The user authors AWS SAM `template.yaml` files for ~30 Lambda services in `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda`. Every one of those templates follows a tightly consistent house style centered on the `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` custom resource (from `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager`), but the conventions live only in tribal knowledge — there is no `Globals` block, no shared base template, and new templates routinely drift (mixed `dns-de-` vs `data-de-` prefixes, mixed runtimes from `python3.8`–`python3.13`, inconsistent tag keys, occasional uses of `LambdaRole` parameter vs `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn`).

This skill encodes those conventions so that when Claude is asked to create or update a SAM `template.yaml`, it produces output that matches the existing repo's style and correctly integrates with `serverless-access-manager`. When editing an existing template, the skill must mirror that template's existing style choices rather than imposing canonical defaults.

## Skill structure

Create the skill at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/skills/sam-template/`:

```
sam-template/
  SKILL.md                  # entry point with YAML frontmatter + decision flow
  reference/
    skeleton.md             # canonical template skeleton + Parameters block
    access-manager.md       # how to wire Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    sources-targets.md      # cheatsheet of Sources/Targets Types and required props
    function-resource.md    # Function pattern: FunctionName, Role, VpcConfig, Layers, ECR ImageUri
    iam-extensions.md       # when/how to attach extra AWS::IAM::Policy or RolePolicy to AccessManager role
    events-and-triggers.md  # AWS::Lambda::Permission patterns for S3/EventBridge; SAM Events: usage
    tags-and-outputs.md     # tag keys, Outputs convention
    examples.md             # pointers to 5 canonical templates in dns-de-automation/lambda
```

Each reference file is short (≤80 lines) and quotes verbatim YAML so the skill body in `SKILL.md` can stay scannable.

## SKILL.md (frontmatter + body)

Frontmatter:
```
name: sam-template
description: Use when the user asks to create or update an AWS SAM template.yaml in the dns-de-automation lambda style, or any SAM template that integrates with serverless-access-manager. Trigger on requests like "add a new lambda", "update template.yaml", "add an SQS target to the access manager".
team: de
```

Body sections (in order):

1. **Decide: new template vs edit existing.** Detect by checking whether `template.yaml` already exists in the target directory. Branch the workflow.
2. **If editing**: read the full existing template first. Detect and lock to the existing style on these axes before changing anything:
   - FunctionName prefix (`dns-de-` vs `data-de-`)
   - Runtime version (do NOT silently upgrade `python3.8` → `python3.13`)
   - Role attachment style (`!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn` vs `!Ref LambdaRole` parameter)
   - Tag key set (`application`, `created_by`, `owner`, `team` — match what's there; do not add tags the file doesn't already use)
   - PackageType (Zip vs Image)
   - Whether `VpcExecution` is `'true'` or `'false'` on the access manager
   - Whether `FunctionName` on the function uses `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName` or a duplicated `!Sub`
   Only fall back to canonical defaults from the reference files for fields the existing template does not address.
3. **If creating new**: use the canonical skeleton from `reference/skeleton.md`. Defaults: `python3.13`, `Timeout: 900`, `MemorySize: 2048`, `data-de-<service>-${Environment}` for FunctionName, `Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn`, `VpcExecution: 'true'`. Ask the user if any of these should differ.
4. **Wire ServerlessAccessManager** per `reference/access-manager.md`: always set `ServiceToken: !Sub 'arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager'`. Use `Sources` for read-only inputs, `Targets` for read/write outputs. Validate every entry's `Type` against the supported list and required `Properties` from `reference/sources-targets.md`. Never inline `Policies:` (SAM policy templates) — use `Sources`/`Targets`, or attach a separate `AWS::IAM::Policy`/`AWS::IAM::RolePolicy` to `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName` for permissions the access manager doesn't model.
5. **Event triggers** per `reference/events-and-triggers.md`: prefer `AWS::Lambda::Permission` resources for S3/EventBridge wiring. SAM-native `Events:` block is rare and only appropriate for SNS topics (cite `zendesk-alerter/template.yaml`).
6. **Outputs**: always emit a single `<FunctionLogicalId>FunctionARN` output with `!GetAtt <FunctionLogicalId>.Arn`. No `Export:`.

# Plan: `sam-template` skill for SAM `template.yaml` authoring

## Context

The user authors AWS SAM `template.yaml` files for ~30 Lambda services in `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda`. Every one of those templates follows a tightly consistent house style centered on the `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` custom resource (from `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager`), but the conventions live only in tribal knowledge — there is no `Globals` block, no shared base template, and new templates routinely drift (mixed `dns-de-` vs `data-de-` prefixes, mixed runtimes from `python3.8`–`python3.13`, inconsistent tag keys, occasional uses of `LambdaRole` parameter vs `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn`).

This skill encodes those conventions so that when Claude is asked to create or update a SAM `template.yaml`, it produces output that matches the existing repo's style and correctly integrates with `serverless-access-manager`. When editing an existing template, the skill must mirror that template's existing style choices rather than imposing canonical defaults.

## Skill structure

Create the skill at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/skills/sam-template/`:

```
sam-template/
  SKILL.md                  # entry point with YAML frontmatter + decision flow
  reference/
    skeleton.md             # canonical template skeleton + Parameters block
    access-manager.md       # how to wire Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    sources-targets.md      # cheatsheet of Sources/Targets Types and required props
    function-resource.md    # Function pattern: FunctionName, Role, VpcConfig, Layers, ECR ImageUri
    iam-extensions.md       # when/how to attach extra AWS::IAM::Policy or RolePolicy to AccessManager role
    events-and-triggers.md  # AWS::Lambda::Permission patterns for S3/EventBridge; SAM Events: usage
    tags-and-outputs.md     # tag keys, Outputs convention
    examples.md             # pointers to 5 canonical templates in dns-de-automation/lambda
```

Each reference file is short (≤80 lines) and quotes verbatim YAML so the skill body in `SKILL.md` can stay scannable.

## SKILL.md (frontmatter + body)

Frontmatter:
```
name: sam-template
description: Use when the user asks to create or update an AWS SAM template.yaml in the dns-de-automation lambda style, or any SAM template that integrates with serverless-access-manager. Trigger on requests like "add a new lambda", "update template.yaml", "add an SQS target to the access manager".
team: de
```

Body sections (in order):

2. **If editing**: read the full existing template first. Detect and lock to the existing style on these axes before changing anything:

   - Tag key set (`application`, `created_by`, `owner`, `team` — match what's there; do not add tags the file doesn't already use)
   - PackageType (Zip vs Image)
   - Whether `VpcExecution` is `'true'` or `'false'` on the access manager
   - Whether `FunctionName` on the function uses `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName` or a duplicated `!Sub`
   Only fall back to canonical defaults from the reference files for fields the existing template does not address.
3. **If creating new**: use the canonical skeleton from `reference/skeleton.md`. Defaults: `python3.13`, `Timeout: 900`, `MemorySize: 2048`, `data-de-<service>-${Environment}` for FunctionName, `Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn`, `VpcExecution: 'true'`. # Plan: `sam-template` skill for SAM `template.yaml` authoring

## Context

The user authors AWS SAM `template.yaml` files for ~30 Lambda services in `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda`. Every one of those templates follows a tightly consistent house style centered on the `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` custom resource (from `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager`), but the conventions live only in tribal knowledge — there is no `Globals` block, no shared base template, and new templates routinely drift (mixed `dns-de-` vs `data-de-` prefixes, mixed runtimes from `python3.8`–`python3.13`, inconsistent tag keys, occasional uses of `LambdaRole` parameter vs `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn`).

This skill encodes those conventions so that when Claude is asked to create or update a SAM `template.yaml`, it produces output that matches the existing repo's style and correctly integrates with `serverless-access-manager`. When editing an existing template, the skill must mirror that template's existing style choices rather than imposing canonical defaults.

## Skill structure

Create the skill at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/skills/sam-template/`:

```
sam-template/
  SKILL.md                  # entry point with YAML frontmatter + decision flow
  reference/
    skeleton.md             # canonical template skeleton + Parameters block
    access-manager.md       # how to wire Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    sources-targets.md      # cheatsheet of Sources/Targets Types and required props
    function-resource.md    # Function pattern: FunctionName, Role, VpcConfig, Layers, ECR ImageUri
    iam-extensions.md       # when/how to attach extra AWS::IAM::Policy or RolePolicy to AccessManager role
    events-and-triggers.md  # AWS::Lambda::Permission patterns for S3/EventBridge; SAM Events: usage
    tags-and-outputs.md     # tag keys, Outputs convention
    examples.md             # pointers to 5 canonical templates in dns-de-automation/lambda
```

Each reference file is short (≤80 lines) and quotes verbatim YAML so the skill body in `SKILL.md` can stay scannable.

## SKILL.md (frontmatter + body)

Frontmatter:
```
name: sam-template
description: Use when the user asks to create or update an AWS SAM template.yaml in the dns-de-automation lambda style, or any SAM template that integrates with serverless-access-manager. Trigger on requests like "add a new lambda", "update template.yaml", "add an SQS target to the access manager".
team: de
```

Body sections (in order):

1. **Decide: new template vs edit existing.** Detect by checking whether `template.yaml` already exists in the target directory. Branch the workflow.
2. **If editing**: read the full existing template first. Detect and lock to the existing style on these axes before changing anything:
   - FunctionName prefix (`dns-de-` vs `data-de-`)
   - Runtime version (do NOT silently upgrade `python3.8` → `python3.13`)
   - Role attachment style (`!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn` vs `!Ref LambdaRole` parameter)
   - Tag key set (`application`, `created_by`, `owner`, `team` — match what's there; do not add tags the file doesn't already use)
   - PackageType (Zip vs Image)
   - Whether `VpcExecution` is `'true'` or `'false'` on the access manager
   - Whether `FunctionName` on the function uses `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName` or a duplicated `!Sub`
   Only fall back to canonical defaults from the reference files for fields the existing template does not address.
3. **If creating new**: use the canonical skeleton from `reference/skeleton.md`. Defaults: `python3.13`, `Timeout: 900`, `MemorySize: 2048`, `data-de-<service>-${Environment}` for FunctionName, `Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn`, `VpcExecution: 'true'`. Ask the user if any of these should differ.
4. **Wire ServerlessAccessManager** per `reference/access-manager.md`: always set `ServiceToken: !Sub 'arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager'`. Use `Sources` for read-only inputs, `Targets` for read/write outputs. Validate every entry's `Type` against the supported list and required `Properties` from `reference/sources-targets.md`. Never inline `Policies:` (SAM policy templates) — use `Sources`/`Targets`, or attach a separate `AWS::IAM::Policy`/`AWS::IAM::RolePolicy` to `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName` for permissions the access manager doesn't model.
5. **Event triggers** per `reference/events-and-triggers.md`: prefer `AWS::Lambda::Permission` resources for S3/EventBridge wiring. SAM-native `Events:` block is rare and only appropriate for SNS topics (cite `zendesk-alerter/template.yaml`).
6. **Outputs**: always emit a single `<FunctionLogicalId>FunctionARN` output with `!GetAtt <FunctionLogicalId>.Arn`. No `Export:`.
7. **Anti-patterns** (call out and refuse): inline `Policies:` with SAM policy templates, `Globals:` block (not used in this repo), hard-coded VPC subnets/security groups (always via `ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig`), missing `Environment` parameter, region pinning to anything other than `eu-west-1`.

## Memory writes (first invocation)

On the first time the skill runs in a conversation, write these memory files via Write tool to `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/projects/-Users-saurabhtyagi--claude/memory/`:

- `project_dns_de_sam_conventions.md` (type: project) — house style: `data-de-<service>-${Environment}` naming, `python3.13` default, `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` is universal, no `Globals` block, region pinned to `eu-west-1`.
- `reference_serverless_access_manager.md` (type: reference) — README at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager/README.md`; ServiceToken pattern; supported Source/Target Types; consumer integration is in-template (no MR to access-manager repo) except cross-account Kinesis (separate MR to `infrastructure` repo).
- `feedback_sam_mirror_existing.md` (type: feedback) — when editing an existing SAM template, mirror its style on FunctionName prefix, runtime, role-attachment style, tag keys, and PackageType. Do not silently upgrade or normalize. **Why:** user explicitly asked for this. **How to apply:** any time `template.yaml` already exists in target dir.

Each entry is also added to `MEMORY.md` index as a one-line pointer. The skill does not rewrite memory on every invocation — it checks whether the entries exist first.

## Canonical examples to cite from `SKILL.md`

These are real, in-repo references the skill body and `examples.md` should link to:

- `lambda/openai-cost-exporter/template.yaml` — minimal zip + EventBridge target + SSM source + tags. Default skeleton.
- `lambda/file-ingestion-framework/template.yaml` — Image-based + multiple S3 targets + EventBridge `AWS::Lambda::Permission`.
- `lambda/ingest-api/template.yaml` — zip + Layer + extra `AWS::IAM::Policy` attached to access-manager role.
- `lambda/event-triggered-ingest/template.yaml` — S3 trigger via `AWS::Lambda::Permission` + EphemeralStorage.
- `lambda/kafka-dynamodb-integrator/template.yaml` — MSK source + DynamoDB target.
- `lambda/zendesk-alerter/template.yaml` — only template using SAM-native `Events:` (SNS) and `DatadogLogging: 'false'`.

## Verification

1. **Skill discoverability**: in a new session, type a phrase like "create a SAM template for a new lambda" or "add a DynamoDB target to template.yaml" and confirm Claude proposes invoking the `sam-template` skill.
2. **New-template golden path**: ask the skill to scaffold a template for a hypothetical service. Diff the output against `lambda/openai-cost-exporter/template.yaml` — fields should align (Parameters set, ServiceToken format, Role attachment, Outputs).
3. **Edit-existing path**: point the skill at `lambda/braze-batcher/template.yaml` (which uses the `LambdaRole` parameter style, NOT `!GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn`) and ask it to add a new SSM Source. Confirm the skill keeps the `LambdaRole` parameter style and does not rewrite the role attachment.
4. **Style mirroring on runtime**: open a template still on `python3.8` (e.g. an older one) and ask the skill to add a new env var. Confirm the runtime is left untouched.
5. **Memory write**: confirm on first invocation that the three memory files appear under the memory directory and that `MEMORY.md` has new index entries. On second invocation in a new session, confirm no duplicates are written.

The skill must also integrate correctly with `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager` from `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/serverless-access-manager`, which all current templates use.

    events-and-triggers.md  # AWS::Lambda::Permission for S3/EventBridge; SAM-native Events block
    tags-and-outputs.md     # tag keys observed in recent templates; Outputs convention

name: sam-template

## Context

The user authors AWS SAM `template.yaml` files for ~30 Lambda services in `/Users/saurabhtyagi/ws/dns-de-automation/lambda`. The repo's templates have drifted over years — runtimes range from `python3.8` to `python3.13`, naming prefixes mix `dns-de-` and `data-de-`, and a handful of templates use older role-attachment styles. The user does NOT want the skill to blindly follow whatever exists in the repo. Instead, the skill should:

1. Use **only templates modified in the last 6 months** (since 2025-11-21, given today is 2026-05-21) as the style reference, since those reflect current house style.
