file.content:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: DNS API Ingestion

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  SupermetricsSecret:
    Description: Secret name for Supermetrics API key
    Type: String
  PropertySecret:
    Description: Secret name for Property API key
    Type: String
  DeCommonLayer:
    Description: ARN of dns-de-common-layer
    Type: String
  MriCredentialSecret:
    Description: Secret name for MRI credentials
    Type: String
  ShopworksTokenSSMParameter:
    Description: SSM Parameter to store Bearer token for Shopworks API endpoints
    Type: String
  HelloCustomerTokenSSMParameter:
    Description: SSM Parameter to store Bearer token for HelloCustomer API endpoints
    Type: String
  HelloCustomerClientCredentialSSMParameter:
    Description: SSM Parameter to store client credentails for HelloCustomer API
    Type: String
  EventBus:
    Description: Name of the Supernova Eventbridge bus
    Type: String
  TeamName:
    Description: Team responsible for application
    Type: String
    Default: de

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'dns-de-api-ingestor-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: 'true'
      Targets:
        DevTargetS3:
          Type: S3
          Properties:
            Bucket: hbi-dns-dev-rdl-staging
        ProdTargetS3:
          Type: S3
          Properties:
            Bucket: hbi-dns-rdl-staging
        Supernova:
          Type: EventBridge
          Properties:
            Bus: !Ref EventBus

  SecretsSSMPermsPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Policy
    Properties:
      PolicyName: api-ingest-secret-ssm-perms
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - 'secretsmanager:DescribeSecret'
              - 'secretsmanager:GetSecretValue'
            Resource: 
              - !Ref SupermetricsSecret
              - !Ref PropertySecret
              - !Ref MriCredentialSecret
          - Effect: Allow
            Action: 'ssm:GetParameter'
            Resource: !Sub arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:parameter/dns/hello_customer/*
          - Effect: Allow
            Action: 'ssm:PutParameter'
            Resource: !Sub arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:parameter/dns/hello_customer/token_*
      Roles:
        - !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName

  APIIngestionLambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Layers:
        - !Ref DeCommonLayer
      Environment:
        Variables:
          Supermetrics_API_Key: !Ref SupermetricsSecret
          Property_API_Key: !Ref PropertySecret
          MRI_credential: !Ref MriCredentialSecret
          Shopworks_token: !Sub '{{resolve:ssm:${ShopworksTokenSSMParameter}}}'
          HelloCustomer_token_Param: !Ref HelloCustomerTokenSSMParameter
          HelloCustomer_clientcreds_Param: !Ref HelloCustomerClientCredentialSSMParameter
          env: !Sub '${Environment}'
      Tags:
        application: 'dns-api-ingestion'
        created_by: cloudformation
        owner: 'de'
        team: !Ref TeamName
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: api_client.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      MemorySize: 10240
      Timeout: 900
      EphemeralStorage:
        Size: 10240


Outputs:
  APIIngestionLambdaFunctionARN:
    Description: "API ingestion lambda function ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt APIIngestionLambdaFunction.Arn

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: OpenAI Cost Exporter

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  TeamName:
    Description: Team responsible for application
    Type: String
    Default: de
  SSMOpenAIAdminKey:
    Description: SSM Parameter for OpenAI Admin Key
    Type: String
  EventBus:
    Description: Name of the Supernova Eventbridge bus
    Type: String

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub 'arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager'
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-de-openai-cost-exporter-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: 'true'
      Targets:
        DevTargetS3:
          Type: S3
          Properties:
            Bucket: hbi-dns-dev-rdl-staging
        ProdTargetS3:
          Type: S3
          Properties:
            Bucket: hbi-dns-rdl-staging
        Supernova:
          Type: EventBridge
          Properties:
            Bus: !Ref EventBus
      Sources:
        SSMParams:
          Type: SSM
          Properties:
            Parameters:
              - /data-engineering/openai/admin-key

  OpenAICostExporterFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Environment:
        Variables:
          Env: !Ref Environment
          OpenAI_Admin_Key: !Ref SSMOpenAIAdminKey
      Tags:
        application: 'openai-cost-exporter'
        created_by: cloudformation
        owner: 'de'
        project: 'openai-cost-exporter'
        product: 'openai-cost-exporter'
        team: !Ref TeamName
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: app.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.12
      MemorySize: 2048
      Timeout: 900

Outputs:
  OpenAICostExporterLambdaFunctionARN:
    Description: "OpenAI Cost Exporter function ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt OpenAICostExporterFunction.Arn

file.content:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: DNS Peronal Data Catalog

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  RetoolPasswordSecretId:
    Description: Secrets Manager secret id for the Retool RDS password
    Type: String
  RetoolUser:
    Description: User name of the retool rds
    Type: String
  RetoolHost:
    Description: Host name of the retool rds
    Type: String
  RetoolDB:
    Description: DB name of the retool rds
    Type: String


Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'dns-de-personal-data-catalog-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: 'true'
      Sources:
        SSMParam:
          Type: SSM
          Properties:
            Parameters:
              - /dns/rte/rds/retool/team_superuser
      Targets:
        ProdRteS3:
          Type: S3
          Properties:
            Bucket: hbi-dns-rte
        DevRteS3:
          Type: S3
          Properties:
            Bucket: hbi-dns-dev-rte

  SecretsManagerPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Policy
    Properties:
      PolicyName: retool-rds-secret-read
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - 'secretsmanager:DescribeSecret'
              - 'secretsmanager:GetSecretValue'
            Resource: !Ref RetoolPasswordSecretId
      Roles:
        - !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName


  PersonalDataCatalogFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Environment:
        Variables:
          Retool_RDS_DB_Password_Secret_Id: !Ref RetoolPasswordSecretId
          Retool_RDS_DB_User: !Ref RetoolUser
          Retool_RDS_DB_Host: !Ref RetoolHost
          Retool_RDS_DB_Name: !Ref RetoolDB
      Tags:
        application: 'personal-data-catalog'
        created_by: cloudformation
        owner: 'de'
        project: 'Right To Erasure'
        team: 'DE'
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: app.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.8
      MemorySize: 2048
      Timeout: 900


Outputs:
  PersonalDataCatalogFunctionARN:
    Description: "Peronal Data Catalog function ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt PersonalDataCatalogFunction.Arn

file.content:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: Metrics Publisher v2

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  TeamName:
    Description: Team responsible for application
    Type: String
    Default: de
  ApplicationName:
    Description: Name of application
    Type: String
    Default: 'metrics-publisher'
  SupernovaLayerVersion:
    Description: Layer version of supernova to use
    Type: String
  Channel:
    Description: Name of Slack Channel to send alerts to
    Type: String
  EventBus:
    Description: Name of the Eventbridge bus
    Type: String
  OTelMetricsEndpoint:
    Description: Endpoint for OTel Collector
    Type: String

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub 'arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager'
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-de-metrics-publisher-v2-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: 'true'
      Targets:
        DevSQS:
          Type: SQS
          Properties:
            Queue: de-supernova-to-metrics-framework-dev
        ProdSQS:
          Type: SQS
          Properties:
            Queue: de-supernova-to-metrics-framework-prod
        Supernova:
          Type: EventBridge
          Properties:
            Bus: !Ref EventBus

  MetricsPublisherV2Function:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Environment:
        Variables:
          Env: !Ref Environment
          SlackChannel: !Ref Channel
          BusName: !Ref EventBus
          OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT: !Ref OTelMetricsEndpoint
          LambdaName: !Sub 'data-de-metrics-publisher-v2-${Environment}'
      Layers:
        - !Ref SupernovaLayerVersion
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: app.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      MemorySize: 2048
      Timeout: 900

Outputs:
  MetricsPublisherV2LambdaFunctionARN:
    Description: "Metrics Publisher v2 function ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt MetricsPublisherV2Function.Arn

content.content:
1	<!-- markdownlint-disable no-inline-html -->
2	# serverless-access-manager
3	
4	> Lambda-backed custom resource to manage per-lambda resources
5	
6	<!-- START doctoc generated TOC please keep comment here to allow auto update -->
7	<!-- DON'T EDIT THIS SECTION, INSTEAD RE-RUN doctoc TO UPDATE -->
8	## Contents
9	
10	- [Background](#background)
11	- [Architecture](#architecture)
12	    - [Overall Process Flow](#overall-process-flow)
13	    - [Input validation and transformation](#input-validation-and-transformation)
14	        - [Tags Validation](#tags-validation)
15	    - [Provisioners](#provisioners)
16	        - [Resource discovery](#resource-discovery)
17	        - [Adding a new provisioner](#adding-a-new-provisioner)
18	    - [Execution order](#execution-order)
19	- [Inputs](#inputs)
20	    - [Sources](#sources)
21	    - [Targets](#targets)
22	- [Attributes](#attributes)
23	- [Examples](#examples)
24	    - [MSK trigger, DynamoDB target](#msk-trigger-dynamodb-target)
25	    - [MSK trigger, DynamoDB target, calls to k8s services](#msk-trigger-dynamodb-target-calls-to-k8s-services)
26	    - [DynamoDB Stream trigger, ElasticSearch target](#dynamodb-stream-trigger-elasticsearch-target)
27	    - [Lambda trigger, EventBridge bus target](#lambda-trigger-eventbridge-bus-target)
28	    - [Chime video trigger, Transcribe output to S3](#chime-video-trigger-transcribe-output-to-s3)
29	    - [Cross-account Kinesis source](#cross-account-kinesis-source)
30	- [Todo](#todo)
31	- [Improvements](#improvements)
32	
33	<!-- END doctoc generated TOC please keep comment here to allow auto update -->
34	
35	---
36	
37	## Background
38	
39	Deployment of Lambda functions to AWS requires additional supporting resources to function correctly, such examples include:
40	
41	- An IAM Role, used as an execution role, that allows access to resources that the code interacts with
42	- VPC Security Group, for functions that need to run within the VPC
43	- Log forwarding to Datadog
44	
45	The purpose of the serverless-access-manager function is to abstract the creation of these resources so that users can pass configuration determining the source/targets of their function, and the necessary underlying resources are provisioned automatically.
46	The *serverless-access-manager* function is invoked as a [Lambda-backed custom resource](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/template-custom-resources-lambda.html), an example use can be seen in the [Examples section](#examples).
47	
48	---
49	
50	## Architecture
51	
52	Underlying resources are managed by their respective provisioner (see [Provisioners](#provisioners)), with all provisioners being fed the configuration so that they can determine the desired resources.
53	The [Custom Resource Helper](https://pypi.org/project/crhelper/) library is used to handle the interaction between the *serverless-access-manager* function and CloudFormation.
54	
55	### Overall Process Flow
56	
57	The diagram below explains how `serverless-access-manager` processes CloudFormation requests:
58	
59	```mermaid
60	sequenceDiagram
61	    participant CF as AWS CloudFormation
62	    participant Lambda as Serverless Access Manager
63	    participant Validator as Input Processor
64	    participant Provisioners as Resource Provisioners
65	
66	    CF->>Lambda: Invoke Serverless Access Manager (Custom Resource)
67	    Lambda->>Validator: Validate Input Data (Tags, Properties)
68	    Validator->>Lambda: Validation Success or Error
69	    Lambda->>Provisioners: Process Input (Define Resources)
70	    Provisioners->>Lambda: Identify Required Resources
71	    Lambda->>Provisioners: Provision or Update Resources
72	    Provisioners->>Lambda: Return Created Resource Info
73	    Lambda->>CF: Send CloudFormation Response
74	```
75	
76	### Input validation and transformation
77	
78	See [Inputs](#inputs) for the required/optional inputs for this custom resource. These inputs are validated and transformed using a high-level processor (`input_processor`) such that all source/target types are aggregated, and the required keys for each (`Type` and `Properties`) are validated. As the properties for each type have different requirements, the validation of the contents of `Properties` is delegated to the relevant provisioner.
79	
80	#### Tags Validation
81	
82	The diagram below explains how stack tags are validated before provisioning starts:
83	
84	```mermaid
85	graph TD;
86	    A[Start Validation] --> B[Extract Stack Tags from CloudFormation]
87	    B --> C{Are Required Tags Present?}
88	
89	    C -- No --> D[Raise InvalidInput: Missing Tags]
90	    C -- Yes --> E{Are All Keys & Values Lowercase?}
91	
92	    E -- No --> F[Raise InvalidInput: Tags Must Be Lowercase]
93	    E -- Yes --> G[Validation Successful!]
94	
95	    D -->|Stop Execution| H[End]
96	    F -->|Stop Execution| H[End]
97	    G -->|Continue| H[End]
98	```
99	
100	### Provisioners
101	
102	Each provisioner is responsible for determining the desired/undesired resources based on the transformed user input, and defining handlers for managing the resources. The `process_input` method is used to process the output of `input_processor.transform()`, using the contents to determine the resources that are desired. Desired resources are then stored in the `self.desired_resources` attribute, to be used during the reconcilation run. The order in which the resources are added to `self.desired_resources` is respected when reconciliation is run. Every key in `self.desired_resources` must have an entry in `self.resources`, which defines the handlers used for the get/set/delete operations.
103	
104	The handlers that can be defined for each resource are as follows:
105	
106	- `get` - Determines the current state of the underlying resource
107	- `set` - Creates the underlying resource
108	- `delete` - Destroys the underlying resource
109	
110	Each provisioner can optionally return attributes that will be made available for other CloudFormation resources to reference using `!GetAtt`. Additionally, provisioners can optionally return `required_permissions`, defining a list of IAM Policy statements that will need to be added to the related IAM Role (managed by the IAM provisioner).
111	
112	Provisioners are not **required** to manage underlying resources, they may also be used to simply define permissions that the function should have, eg. dynamodb provisions no resources, but returns an IAM policy statement that allows access to an existing resource. In such cases, only the `set` handler needs to exist for the resource, the `get` and `delete` handlers do not need to exist.
113	
114	#### Resource discovery
115	
116	Underlying resources are determined to be managed by *serverless-access-manager* through the use of standard naming, and tagging.
117	The standard naming of a underlying resource is defined by the `resource_id` property in `self.resources.<resource>`. The `resource_id` should be set to a unique, deterministic, attribute of the underlying resource; eg. Cloudwatch Log Groups cannot have the same name, so this should be used as the `resource_id`.
118	The tags that are expected to be attached to the underlying resource are `created_by` and `cloudformation:stack-id` (defined in [BaseProvisioner](src/serverless_access_manager/provisioners/base.py) `self.required_management_tags_template`).
119	
120	If an existing resource matches the standard naming and has the expected tags, it is determined to be managed by *serverless-access-manager*. If an existing resource matches the standard naming and **does not** have the expected tags, the resource is not managed by *serverless-access-manager*, so the `get` operator should be configured to fail.
121	
122	#### Adding a new provisioner
123	
124	Every provisioner must:
125	
126	- Extend the `BaseProvisioner` class
127	- Override the `process_input` and `__init__` methods
128	- Define the handler(s) that manage the resources for the provisioner
129	- Set the `self.resources` map that defines these handlers
130	
131	### Execution order
132	
133	To ensure stability of the underlying resources, validation is carried out prior to any changes being made. This is done by following the below flow:
134	
135	1. `process_input` is invoked for all provisioners
136	2. All `get` operators are invoked for all provisioners
137	3. Each provisioner then runs the reconciliation (create/cleanup) for the desired/undesired resources, before moving on to the next resource
138	
139	---
140	
141	## Inputs
142	
143	This automation requires the `FunctionName` property to be defined, which is used to identify the underlying resources for the related function. The following properties are optional:
144	
145	- `DatadogLogging` - Enables log forwarding to Datadog, set to `"false"` to disable (defaults to `"true"`).
146	- `Sources` - Map of resources to enable readonly access for. See [sources](#sources) for available sources.
147	- `Targets` - Map of resources to enable readwrite access for. See [targets](#targets) for available targets.
148	- `VpcExecution` - **Forces** the creation of a Security Group, and permissions required for the function to run in the VPC. Set to `"true"` if VPC execution is required but not enabled implicitly from requested sources/targets (defaults to `"false"`)
149	
150	---
151	
152	### Sources
153	
154	| Type | Properties | Notes |
155	| ---- | ---------- | ----- |
156	| Connect | `Alias` | Alias of Connect instance to get read access of contact attributes |
157	| DynamoDB | `Table` | Optional. Name of table to get read access to. |
158	| | `StreamArn` | Optional. ARN of DynamoDB Stream to be triggered by. |
159	| ElasticSearch | `Cluster` | Name of cluster to get network access to (enables VPC execution). |
160	| Kinesis | `Stream` | Name of the stream to get readonly access to. |
161	| Lex | `BotID` | ID of the bot to get readonly access to intents. |
162	| MSK  | `Topics` | List of Kafka topics to be triggered by. |
163	| S3  | `Bucket` | Name of S3 bucket to get readonly access to. |
164	| SNS  | `Topic` | Name of SNS topic to get subscribe access to. |
165	| SQS  | `Queue` | Name of SQS queue to be triggered by. Receives message read and delete permissions. |
166	| SSM  | `Parameters` | List of SSM Parameters to get readonly access to. Limited Support for the these path prefixes: `["/corp-sys/", "/sc-spange/", "/dns/", "/datascience/"]` |
167	| Transcribe | `bucket_name`, `input_prefix` | Grants read access to video files for transcription |
168	
169	---
170	
171	### Targets
172	
173	| Type | Properties | Notes |
174	| ---- | ---------- | ----- |
175	| Connect | `Alias` | Alias of the Connect instance to be able to create participants for. |
176	| DynamoDB | `Table` | Name of table to get read-write access to. |
177	| ElasticSearch | `Cluster` | Name of cluster to get network access to (enables VPC execution). |
178	| EventBridge  | `Bus` | Name of the Event bus to write events to. |
179	| LambdaFunctionUrl  | `Functions` | List of functions URLs to be able to invoke. `AWS_IAM` auth only. |
180	| MSK  | `Topics` | List of Kafka topics to write to (enables VPC execution). |
181	| S3  | `Bucket` | Name of S3 bucket to get readwrite access to. |
182	| SNS  | `Topic` | Name of SNS topic to get publish access to. |
183	| SQS  | `Queue` | Name of SQS queue to get consume and publish access to. |
184	| Transcribe | `bucket_name`, `output_prefix` | Grants write access for transcription output |
185	
186	---
187	
188	## Attributes
189	
190	The following attributes can be referenced using the `!GetAtt` function.
191	
192	| Attribute | Availability | Notes |
193	| --------- | ---------- | ----- |
194	| `FunctionName` | Always | Same value as `FunctionName` input. Can be used to deduplicate strings in CloudFormation template. |
195	| `LambdaExecutionRoleArn` | Always | ARN of IAM Role to attach to the Lambda Function. |
196	| `LambdaExecutionRoleName` | Always | Friendly name of IAM Role to attach to the Lambda Function. |
197	| `VpcConfig` | When VPC execution is enabled | Map of configuration required for VPC-based execution. |
198	| `VpcSgId` | When VPC execution is enabled | ID of Security Group for VPC-based execution. |
199	
200	---
201	
202	## Examples
203	
204	**NOTE**: These examples do not include the **creation** of the event trigger, this needs to be configured separately.
205	
206	### MSK trigger, DynamoDB target
207	
208	Triggered by kafka topic `myteam-topic`, writes items to a DynamoDB table `myteam-table`. Makes no other calls, so does not need to be in the VPC.
209	
210	<details>
211	<summary>Example Template</summary>
212	
213	```yaml
214	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
215	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
216	Description: My function
217	
218	Resources:
219	  ServerlessAccessManager:
220	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
221	    Properties:
222	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
223	      FunctionName: myteam-function
224	      Sources:
225	        MyTopic:
226	          Type: MSK
227	          Properties:
228	            Topics:
229	              - myteam-topic
230	      Targets:
231	        MyDynamoTable:
232	          Type: DynamoDB
233	          Properties:
234	            Table: myteam-table
235	
236	  HelloWorldLambda:
237	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
238	    Properties:
239	      FunctionName: myteam-function
240	      CodeUri: src/
241	      Handler: hello_world.lambda_handler
242	      Runtime: python3.12
243	      MemorySize: 128
244	      Timeout: 6
245	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
246	
247	Outputs:
248	  IamRoleArn:
249	    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
250	    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
251	```
252	
253	</details>
254	
255	### MSK trigger, DynamoDB target, calls to k8s services
256	
257	Triggered by kafka topic `myteam-topic`, writes items to a DynamoDB table `myteam-table`. Makes calls to kubernetes service, so needs to be in the VPC.
258	
259	<details>
260	<summary>Example Template</summary>
261	
262	```yaml
263	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
264	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
265	Description: My function
266	
267	Resources:
268	  ServerlessAccessManager:
269	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
270	    Properties:
271	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
272	      FunctionName: myteam-function
273	      VpcExecution: true
274	      Sources:
275	        MyTopic:
276	          Type: MSK
277	          Properties:
278	            Topics:
279	              - myteam-topic
280	      Targets:
281	        MyDynamoTable:
282	          Type: DynamoDB
283	          Properties:
284	            Table: myteam-table
285	
286	  HelloWorldLambda:
287	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
288	    Properties:
289	      FunctionName: myteam-function
290	      CodeUri: src/
291	      Handler: hello_world.lambda_handler
292	      Runtime: python3.12
293	      MemorySize: 128
294	      Timeout: 6
295	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
296	      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
297	
298	Outputs:
299	  IamRoleArn:
300	    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
301	    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
302	```
303	
304	</details>
305	
306	---
307	
308	### DynamoDB Stream trigger, ElasticSearch target
309	
310	Triggered by DynamoDB Stream `arn:aws:dynamodb:eu-west-1:123456789012:table/myteam-table/stream/abcdef1234567890`, writes items to a ElasticSearch cluster `myteam-search`. ElasticSearch requires internal network access, so needs to be in the VPC.
311	
312	<details>
313	<summary>Example Template</summary>
314	
315	```yaml
316	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
317	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
318	Description: My function
319	
320	Resources:
321	  ServerlessAccessManager:
322	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
323	    Properties:
324	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
325	      FunctionName: myteam-function
326	      Sources:
327	        MyStream:
328	          Type: DynamoDB
329	          Properties:
330	            StreamArn: arn:aws:dynamodb:eu-west-1:123456789012:table/myteam-table/stream/abcdef1234567890
331	      Targets:
332	        MyEsCluster:
333	          Type: ElasticSearch
334	          Properties:
335	            Cluster: myteam-search
336	
337	  HelloWorldLambda:
338	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
339	    Properties:
340	      FunctionName: myteam-function
341	      CodeUri: src/
342	      Handler: hello_world.lambda_handler
343	      Runtime: python3.12
344	      MemorySize: 128
345	      Timeout: 6
346	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
347	      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
348	
349	Outputs:
350	  IamRoleArn:
351	    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
352	    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
353	```
354	
355	</details>
356	
357	---
358	
359	### Lambda trigger, EventBridge bus target
360	
361	Triggered by Lambda invocation: a dummy event(s) is `put` to a EventBridge Event Bus `myteam-bus`. Permissions granted by [serverless-access-manager](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/platform-as-a-service/serverless-applications/serverless-access-manager#targets)
362	
363	Checkout this [example repo](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/platform-as-a-service/examples/sam-lambda-to-eventbridge-demo) for a working example that deploys and tests this functionality.
364	
365	<details>
366	<summary>Example Template</summary>
367	
368	```yaml
369	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
370	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
371	Description: Lambda function with permissions to push Events to a target EventBridge bus
372	
373	Resources:
374	  ServerlessAccessManager:
375	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
376	    Properties:
377	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
378	      FunctionName: !Sub "<my-team>-event-pusher"
379	      Targets:
380	        MyBus:
381	          Type: Eventbridge
382	          Properties:
383	            Bus: <team>-bus # Define target bus
384	
385	
386	  LambdaEventPusher:
387	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
388	    Properties:
389	      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
390	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
391	      Tags:
392	        application: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
393	        created_by: cloudformation
394	        owner: <my-team>
395	      CodeUri: src/
396	      Handler: app.handler
397	      Runtime: python3.12
398	      MemorySize: 128
399	      Timeout: 6
400	      Environment:
401	        Variables:
402	          LOG_LEVEL: debug
403	
404	Outputs:
405	  IamRoleArn:
406	    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
407	    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
408	```
409	
410	</details>
411	
412	---
413	
414	### Chime video trigger, Transcribe output to S3
415	
416	> ⚠ **Note on AWS Transcribe connectivity:**
417	>
418	> AWS Transcribe is a **public AWS service** and must be accessed via the public internet or through a PrivateLink (VPC Interface Endpoint).
419	> If your Lambda function runs in a **private subnet without a NAT Gateway or PrivateLink**, the transcription job will **timeout silently**.
420	> Please contact the **PaaS team** to ensure that Transcribe PrivateLink access is configured for your environment.
421	>
422	> ⚠ **Note:**
423	>
424	> - The `input_prefix` is used to scope IAM permissions within the specified bucket. It must reflect the path structure of your recordings, including dynamic folders like meeting IDs.
425	>   For example: `input_prefix: "*/concatenated/composited-video/*.mp4"` will allow access to objects like `meeting123/concatenated/composited-video/video.mp4` inside the same bucket.
426	>
427	> - The `output_prefix` determines where AWS Transcribe is allowed to write its results. Use a wildcard to match dynamic meeting folders:
428	>
429	>   `output_prefix: "*/concatenated/composited-video/transcripts/*"`
430	>
431	>   This grants write access to results like:
432	>   `s3://chime-meeting-recordings/<meeting-id>/concatenated/composited-video/transcripts/output.json`
433	>
434	> - AWS Transcribe writes a temporary file named `.write_access_check_file.temp` to the `output_prefix` path before beginning the transcription job.
435	>   To ensure success, you should grant write access to the **entire output folder**, not just `.json` results.
436	>
437	
438	When a new `.mp4` file is uploaded to a Chime meeting folder, an S3 event triggers a Lambda function, which starts a Transcribe job. The resulting transcription is written by AWS Transcribe to a specified location in the same S3 bucket.
439	
440	<details>
441	<summary>Example Template</summary>
442	
443	```yaml
444	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
445	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
446	Description: Transcribe job setup with S3 access
447	
448	Resources:
449	  ServerlessAccessManager:
450	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
451	    Properties:
452	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
453	      FunctionName: chime-transcribe-function
454	      Sources:
455	        InputVideo:
456	          Type: Transcribe
457	          Properties:
458	            bucket_name: chime-meeting-recordings
459	            input_prefix: "*/concatenated/composited-video/*.mp4"
460	      Targets:
461	        OutputTranscript:
462	          Type: Transcribe
463	          Properties:
464	            bucket_name: chime-meeting-recordings
465	            output_prefix: "*/concatenated/composited-video/transcripts/*"
466	
467	  TranscribeFunction:
468	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
469	    Properties:
470	      FunctionName: chime-transcribe-function
471	      CodeUri: src/
472	      Handler: transcribe.handler
473	      Runtime: python3.12
474	      Timeout: 10
475	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
476	
477	Outputs:
478	  RoleArn:
479	    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
480	```
481	
482	</details>
483	
484	---
485	
486	### Cross-account Kinesis source
487	
488	This will allow your Lambda to read from the given stream. Support for cross-account access is available by providing the full ARN of the stream e.g. `my-test-stream`, but if the stream is in the same account, simply provide the stream name `my-test-stream`
489	
490	This alone will **not** allow the Lambda to be triggered by the stream. If you wish it to be triggered by the stream, please follow the instructions below
491	
492	<details>
493	<summary>Trigger Lambda Instructions</summary>
494	
495	1. Add the following statement to the template below and deploy. This tells the Lambda that the stream is allowed to invoke it
496	
497	    ```yaml
498	    ExampleApplicationLambdaPermission:
499	      Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
500	      Properties:
501	        FunctionName: !Ref ExampleApplicationLambda
502	        Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
503	        Principal: kinesis.amazonaws.com
504	        SourceAccount: "<account-id>" # Replace with the account ID of the stream
505	        SourceArn: arn:aws:kinesis:eu-west-1:<account-id>:stream/<your-stream-name>
506	    ```
507	
508	2. In the [infrastructure](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/platform-as-a-service/infrastructure) repo take the execution role ARN, which is the output shown in the example below, and add it to the `Principals` of the Kinesis stream. This gives a trust between the Kinesis stream and the Lambda to allow the Lambda to read from the stream
509	
510	    In `kinesis/<env>/<region>/<your-stream>/terraform.tfvars`. The policy should already be there, so just add to the `Principal` list.
511	
512	    ```terraform
513	    config = {
514	      name = "my-stream-test"
515	      policy = <<EOF
516	    {
517	      "Version": "2012-10-17",
518	      "Statement": [{
519	        "Effect": "Allow",
520	        "Principal": {
521	          "AWS": [
522	            "arn:aws:iam::<account-id>:role/lambda-execution-roles/<lambda-execution-role>"
523	          ]
524	        },
525	        "Action": [
526	          "kinesis:DescribeStreamSummary",
527	          "kinesis:GetShardIterator",
528	          "kinesis:DescribeStream",
529	          "kinesis:ListShards",
530	          "kinesis:PutRecord",
531	          "kinesis:PutRecords",
532	          "kinesis:GetRecords"
533	        ],
534	        "Resource": "this-resource-arn"
535	      }]
536	    }
537	    EOF
538	    }
539	    ```
540	
541	3. In the same file, add to the `lambda_event_source_mappings`. This is the trigger from the stream to the Lambda. You only need to add the `stream_arn` if the stream is in another account, for cross-account access.
542	
543	    ```terraform
544	    lambda_event_source_mappings = [
545	      {
546	        function_name = "dso-dhendry-sls-testing"
547	        stream_arn    = "arn:aws:kinesis:eu-west-1:565687767411:stream/kinesis-dso-test-stream" # Only add this line the stream is NOT in the same account as the Lambda.
548	      }
549	    ]
550	    ```
551	
552	4. Submit the MR for the above and your Lambda trigger is ready.
553	
554	</details>
555	
556	<details>
557	<summary>Example Template</summary>
558	
559	```yaml
560	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
561	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
562	Description: Lambda function with permissions to push Events to a target EventBridge bus
563	
564	Resources:
565	  ServerlessAccessManager:
566	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
567	    Properties:
568	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
569	      FunctionName: !Sub "<my-team>-event-pusher"
570	      Sources:
571	        test-stream:
572	          Type: Kinesis
573	          Properties:
574	            # If the stream is in the same account, just put the stream name e.g,
575	            # Stream: kinesis-dso-test-stream
576	            Stream: arn:aws:kinesis:eu-west-1:565687767411:stream/kinesis-dso-test-stream
577	
578	  LambdaEventPusher:
579	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
580	    Properties:
581	      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
582	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
583	      Tags:
584	        application: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
585	        created_by: cloudformation
586	        owner: <my-team>
587	      CodeUri: src/
588	      Handler: app.handler
589	      Runtime: python3.13
590	      MemorySize: 128
591	      Timeout: 6
592	      Environment:
593	        Variables:
594	          LOG_LEVEL: debug
595	
596	Outputs:
597	  IamRoleArn:
598	    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
599	    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
600	```
601	
602	</details>
603	
604	---
605	
606	## Todo
607	
608	- Tests (IAM, MSK, DynamoDB, ElasticSearch)
609	- Figure out common log config (still has duplicates)
610	
611	## Improvements
612	
613	- Ability to specify optional tags to attach to resources created by function
614	    - This can be done by adding optional tags (for reconcile) to render_tags()
615	- Ability to update tags on already created resources
616	- Auto discovery of provisioners
617

# serverless-access-manager

> Lambda-backed custom resource to manage per-lambda resources

<!-- START doctoc generated TOC please keep comment here to allow auto update -->
<!-- DON'T EDIT THIS SECTION, INSTEAD RE-RUN doctoc TO UPDATE -->
## Contents

- [Background](#background)
- [Architecture](#architecture)
    - [Overall Process Flow](#overall-process-flow)
    - [Input validation and transformation](#input-validation-and-transformation)
        - [Tags Validation](#tags-validation)
    - [Provisioners](#provisioners)

## Background

Deployment of Lambda functions to AWS requires additional supporting resources to function correctly, such examples include:

- An IAM Role, used as an execution role, that allows access to resources that the code interacts with
- VPC Security Group, for functions that need to run within the VPC
- Log forwarding to Datadog

The purpose of the serverless-access-manager function is to abstract the creation of these resources so that users can pass configuration determining the source/targets of their function, and the necessary underlying resources are provisioned automatically.
The *serverless-access-manager* function is invoked as a [Lambda-backed custom resource](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/template-custom-resources-lambda.html), an example use can be seen in the [Examples section](#examples).

---

## Architecture

Underlying resources are managed by their respective provisioner (see [Provisioners](#provisioners)), with all provisioners being fed the configuration so that they can determine the desired resources.
The [Custom Resource Helper](https://pypi.org/project/crhelper/) library is used to handle the interaction between the *serverless-access-manager* function and CloudFormation.

### Overall Process Flow

The diagram below explains how `serverless-access-manager` processes CloudFormation requests:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CF as AWS CloudFormation
    participant Lambda as Serverless Access Manager
    participant Validator as Input Processor
    participant Provisioners as Resource Provisioners

    CF->>Lambda: Invoke Serverless Access Manager (Custom Resource)
    Lambda->>Validator: Validate Input Data (Tags, Properties)
    Validator->>Lambda: Validation Success or Error
    Lambda->>Provisioners: Process Input (Define Resources)
    Provisioners->>Lambda: Identify Required Resources
    Lambda->>Provisioners: Provision or Update Resources
    Provisioners->>Lambda: Return Created Resource Info
    Lambda->>CF: Send CloudFormation Response
```

### Input validation and transformation

See [Inputs](#inputs) for the required/optional inputs for this custom resource. These inputs are validated and transformed using a high-level processor (`input_processor`) such that all source/target types are aggregated, and the required keys for each (`Type` and `Properties`) are validated. As the properties for each type have different requirements, the validation of the contents of `Properties` is delegated to the relevant provisioner.

#### Tags Validation

The diagram below explains how stack tags are validated before provisioning starts:

```mermaid
graph TD;
    A[Start Validation] --> B[Extract Stack Tags from CloudFormation]
    B --> C{Are Required Tags Present?}

    C -- No --> D[Raise InvalidInput: Missing Tags]
    C -- Yes --> E{Are All Keys & Values Lowercase?}

    E -- No --> F[Raise InvalidInput: Tags Must Be Lowercase]
    E -- Yes --> G[Validation Successful!]

    D -->|Stop Execution| H[End]
    F -->|Stop Execution| H[End]
    G -->|Continue| H[End]
```

### Provisioners

Each provisioner is responsible for determining the desired/undesired resources based on the transformed user input, and defining handlers for managing the resources. The `process_input` method is used to process the output of `input_processor.transform()`, using the contents to determine the resources that are desired. Desired resources are then stored in the `self.desired_resources` attribute, to be used during the reconcilation run. The order in which the resources are added to `self.desired_resources` is respected when reconciliation is run. Every key in `self.desired_resources` must have an entry in `self.resources`, which defines the handlers used for the get/set/delete operations.

The handlers that can be defined for each resource are as follows:

- `get` - Determines the current state of the underlying resource
- `set` - Creates the underlying resource
- `delete` - Destroys the underlying resource

Each provisioner can optionally return attributes that will be made available for other CloudFormation resources to reference using `!GetAtt`. Additionally, provisioners can optionally return `required_permissions`, defining a list of IAM Policy statements that will need to be added to the related IAM Role (managed by the IAM provisioner).

Provisioners are not **required** to manage underlying resources, they may also be used to simply define permissions that the function should have, eg. dynamodb provisions no resources, but returns an IAM policy statement that allows access to an existing resource. In such cases, only the `set` handler needs to exist for the resource, the `get` and `delete` handlers do not need to exist.

#### Resource discovery

Underlying resources are determined to be managed by *serverless-access-manager* through the use of standard naming, and tagging.
The standard naming of a underlying resource is defined by the `resource_id` property in `self.resources.<resource>`. The `resource_id` should be set to a unique, deterministic, attribute of the underlying resource; eg. Cloudwatch Log Groups cannot have the same name, so this should be used as the `resource_id`.
The tags that are expected to be attached to the underlying resource are `created_by` and `cloudformation:stack-id` (defined in [BaseProvisioner](src/serverless_access_manager/provisioners/base.py) `self.required_management_tags_template`).

If an existing resource matches the standard naming and has the expected tags, it is determined to be managed by *serverless-access-manager*. If an existing resource matches the standard naming and **does not** have the expected tags, the resource is not managed by *serverless-access-manager*, so the `get` operator should be configured to fail.

#### Adding a new provisioner

Every provisioner must:

- Extend the `BaseProvisioner` class
- Override the `process_input` and `__init__` methods
- Define the handler(s) that manage the resources for the provisioner
- Set the `self.resources` map that defines these handlers

### Execution order

To ensure stability of the underlying resources, validation is carried out prior to any changes being made. This is done by following the below flow:

1. `process_input` is invoked for all provisioners
2. All `get` operators are invoked for all provisioners
3. Each provisioner then runs the reconciliation (create/cleanup) for the desired/undesired resources, before moving on to the next resource

---

## Inputs

This automation requires the `FunctionName` property to be defined, which is used to identify the underlying resources for the related function. The following properties are optional:

- `DatadogLogging` - Enables log forwarding to Datadog, set to `"false"` to disable (defaults to `"true"`).
- `Sources` - Map of resources to enable readonly access for. See [sources](#sources) for available sources.
- `Targets` - Map of resources to enable readwrite access for. See [targets](#targets) for available targets.
- `VpcExecution` - **Forces** the creation of a Security Group, and permissions required for the function to run in the VPC. Set to `"true"` if VPC execution is required but not enabled implicitly from requested sources/targets (defaults to `"false"`)

---

### Sources

| Type | Properties | Notes |
| ---- | ---------- | ----- |
| Connect | `Alias` | Alias of Connect instance to get read access of contact attributes |
| DynamoDB | `Table` | Optional. Name of table to get read access to. |
| | `StreamArn` | Optional. ARN of DynamoDB Stream to be triggered by. |
| ElasticSearch | `Cluster` | Name of cluster to get network access to (enables VPC execution). |
| Kinesis | `Stream` | Name of the stream to get readonly access to. |
| Lex | `BotID` | ID of the bot to get readonly access to intents. |
| MSK  | `Topics` | List of Kafka topics to be triggered by. |
| S3  | `Bucket` | Name of S3 bucket to get readonly access to. |
| SNS  | `Topic` | Name of SNS topic to get subscribe access to. |
| SQS  | `Queue` | Name of SQS queue to be triggered by. Receives message read and delete permissions. |
| SSM  | `Parameters` | List of SSM Parameters to get readonly access to. Limited Support for the these path prefixes: `["/corp-sys/", "/sc-spange/", "/dns/", "/datascience/"]` |
| Transcribe | `bucket_name`, `input_prefix` | Grants read access to video files for transcription |

---

### Targets

| Type | Properties | Notes |
| ---- | ---------- | ----- |
| Connect | `Alias` | Alias of the Connect instance to be able to create participants for. |
| DynamoDB | `Table` | Name of table to get read-write access to. |
| ElasticSearch | `Cluster` | Name of cluster to get network access to (enables VPC execution). |
| EventBridge  | `Bus` | Name of the Event bus to write events to. |
| LambdaFunctionUrl  | `Functions` | List of functions URLs to be able to invoke. `AWS_IAM` auth only. |
| MSK  | `Topics` | List of Kafka topics to write to (enables VPC execution). |
| S3  | `Bucket` | Name of S3 bucket to get readwrite access to. |
| SNS  | `Topic` | Name of SNS topic to get publish access to. |
| SQS  | `Queue` | Name of SQS queue to get consume and publish access to. |
| Transcribe | `bucket_name`, `output_prefix` | Grants write access for transcription output |

---

## Attributes

The following attributes can be referenced using the `!GetAtt` function.

| Attribute | Availability | Notes |
| --------- | ---------- | ----- |
| `FunctionName` | Always | Same value as `FunctionName` input. Can be used to deduplicate strings in CloudFormation template. |
| `LambdaExecutionRoleArn` | Always | ARN of IAM Role to attach to the Lambda Function. |
| `LambdaExecutionRoleName` | Always | Friendly name of IAM Role to attach to the Lambda Function. |
| `VpcConfig` | When VPC execution is enabled | Map of configuration required for VPC-based execution. |
| `VpcSgId` | When VPC execution is enabled | ID of Security Group for VPC-based execution. |

---

## Examples

**NOTE**: These examples do not include the **creation** of the event trigger, this needs to be configured separately.

### MSK trigger, DynamoDB target

Triggered by kafka topic `myteam-topic`, writes items to a DynamoDB table `myteam-table`. Makes no other calls, so does not need to be in the VPC.

<details>
<summary>Example Template</summary>

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: My function

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      Sources:
        MyTopic:
          Type: MSK
          Properties:
            Topics:
              - myteam-topic
      Targets:
        MyDynamoTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: myteam-table

  HelloWorldLambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: hello_world.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.12
      MemorySize: 128
      Timeout: 6
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn

Outputs:
  IamRoleArn:
    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
```

</details>

### MSK trigger, DynamoDB target, calls to k8s services

Triggered by kafka topic `myteam-topic`, writes items to a DynamoDB table `myteam-table`. Makes calls to kubernetes service, so needs to be in the VPC.

<details>
<summary>Example Template</summary>

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: My function

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      VpcExecution: true
      Sources:
        MyTopic:
          Type: MSK
          Properties:
            Topics:
              - myteam-topic
      Targets:
        MyDynamoTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: myteam-table

  HelloWorldLambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: hello_world.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.12
      MemorySize: 128
      Timeout: 6
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig

Outputs:
  IamRoleArn:
    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
```

</details>

### DynamoDB Stream trigger, ElasticSearch target

Triggered by DynamoDB Stream `arn:aws:dynamodb:eu-west-1:123456789012:table/myteam-table/stream/abcdef1234567890`, writes items to a ElasticSearch cluster `myteam-search`. ElasticSearch requires internal network access, so needs to be in the VPC.

<details>
<summary>Example Template</summary>

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: My function

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      Sources:
        MyStream:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            StreamArn: arn:aws:dynamodb:eu-west-1:123456789012:table/myteam-table/stream/abcdef1234567890
      Targets:
        MyEsCluster:
          Type: ElasticSearch
          Properties:
            Cluster: myteam-search

  HelloWorldLambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: myteam-function
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: hello_world.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.12
      MemorySize: 128
      Timeout: 6
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig

Outputs:
  IamRoleArn:
    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
```

</details>

### Lambda trigger, EventBridge bus target

Triggered by Lambda invocation: a dummy event(s) is `put` to a EventBridge Event Bus `myteam-bus`. Permissions granted by [serverless-access-manager](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/platform-as-a-service/serverless-applications/serverless-access-manager#targets)

Checkout this [example repo](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/platform-as-a-service/examples/sam-lambda-to-eventbridge-demo) for a working example that deploys and tests this functionality.

<details>
<summary>Example Template</summary>

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: Lambda function with permissions to push Events to a target EventBridge bus

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub "<my-team>-event-pusher"
      Targets:
        MyBus:
          Type: Eventbridge
          Properties:
            Bus: <team>-bus # Define target bus


  LambdaEventPusher:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Tags:
        application: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
        created_by: cloudformation
        owner: <my-team>
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: app.handler
      Runtime: python3.12
      MemorySize: 128
      Timeout: 6
      Environment:
        Variables:
          LOG_LEVEL: debug

Outputs:
  IamRoleArn:
    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
```

</details>

### Chime video trigger, Transcribe output to S3

> ⚠ **Note on AWS Transcribe connectivity:**
>
> AWS Transcribe is a **public AWS service** and must be accessed via the public internet or through a PrivateLink (VPC Interface Endpoint).
> If your Lambda function runs in a **private subnet without a NAT Gateway or PrivateLink**, the transcription job will **timeout silently**.
> Please contact the **PaaS team** to ensure that Transcribe PrivateLink access is configured for your environment.
>
> ⚠ **Note:**
>
> - The `input_prefix` is used to scope IAM permissions within the specified bucket. It must reflect the path structure of your recordings, including dynamic folders like meeting IDs.
>   For example: `input_prefix: "*/concatenated/composited-video/*.mp4"` will allow access to objects like `meeting123/concatenated/composited-video/video.mp4` inside the same bucket.
>
> - The `output_prefix` determines where AWS Transcribe is allowed to write its results. Use a wildcard to match dynamic meeting folders:
>
>   `output_prefix: "*/concatenated/composited-video/transcripts/*"`
>
>   This grants write access to results like:
>   `s3://chime-meeting-recordings/<meeting-id>/concatenated/composited-video/transcripts/output.json`
>
> - AWS Transcribe writes a temporary file named `.write_access_check_file.temp` to the `output_prefix` path before beginning the transcription job.
>   To ensure success, you should grant write access to the **entire output folder**, not just `.json` results.
>

When a new `.mp4` file is uploaded to a Chime meeting folder, an S3 event triggers a Lambda function, which starts a Transcribe job. The resulting transcription is written by AWS Transcribe to a specified location in the same S3 bucket.

<details>
<summary>Example Template</summary>

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: Transcribe job setup with S3 access

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: chime-transcribe-function
      Sources:
        InputVideo:
          Type: Transcribe
          Properties:
            bucket_name: chime-meeting-recordings
            input_prefix: "*/concatenated/composited-video/*.mp4"
      Targets:
        OutputTranscript:
          Type: Transcribe
          Properties:
            bucket_name: chime-meeting-recordings
            output_prefix: "*/concatenated/composited-video/transcripts/*"

  TranscribeFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: chime-transcribe-function
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: transcribe.handler
      Runtime: python3.12
      Timeout: 10
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn

Outputs:
  RoleArn:
    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
```

</details>

### Cross-account Kinesis source

This will allow your Lambda to read from the given stream. Support for cross-account access is available by providing the full ARN of the stream e.g. `my-test-stream`, but if the stream is in the same account, simply provide the stream name `my-test-stream`

This alone will **not** allow the Lambda to be triggered by the stream. If you wish it to be triggered by the stream, please follow the instructions below

<details>
<summary>Trigger Lambda Instructions</summary>

1. Add the following statement to the template below and deploy. This tells the Lambda that the stream is allowed to invoke it

    ```yaml
    ExampleApplicationLambdaPermission:
      Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
      Properties:
        FunctionName: !Ref ExampleApplicationLambda
        Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
        Principal: kinesis.amazonaws.com
        SourceAccount: "<account-id>" # Replace with the account ID of the stream
        SourceArn: arn:aws:kinesis:eu-west-1:<account-id>:stream/<your-stream-name>
    ```

2. In the [infrastructure](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/platform-as-a-service/infrastructure) repo take the execution role ARN, which is the output shown in the example below, and add it to the `Principals` of the Kinesis stream. This gives a trust between the Kinesis stream and the Lambda to allow the Lambda to read from the stream

    In `kinesis/<env>/<region>/<your-stream>/terraform.tfvars`. The policy should already be there, so just add to the `Principal` list.

    ```terraform
    config = {
      name = "my-stream-test"
      policy = <<EOF
    {
      "Version": "2012-10-17",
      "Statement": [{
        "Effect": "Allow",
        "Principal": {
          "AWS": [
            "arn:aws:iam::<account-id>:role/lambda-execution-roles/<lambda-execution-role>"
          ]
        },
        "Action": [
          "kinesis:DescribeStreamSummary",
          "kinesis:GetShardIterator",
          "kinesis:DescribeStream",
          "kinesis:ListShards",
          "kinesis:PutRecord",
          "kinesis:PutRecords",
          "kinesis:GetRecords"
        ],
        "Resource": "this-resource-arn"
      }]
    }
    EOF
    }
    ```

3. In the same file, add to the `lambda_event_source_mappings`. This is the trigger from the stream to the Lambda. You only need to add the `stream_arn` if the stream is in another account, for cross-account access.

    ```terraform
    lambda_event_source_mappings = [
      {
        function_name = "dso-dhendry-sls-testing"
        stream_arn    = "arn:aws:kinesis:eu-west-1:565687767411:stream/kinesis-dso-test-stream" # Only add this line the stream is NOT in the same account as the Lambda.
      }
    ]
    ```

4. Submit the MR for the above and your Lambda trigger is ready.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Example Template</summary>

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: Lambda function with permissions to push Events to a target EventBridge bus

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub "<my-team>-event-pusher"
      Sources:
        test-stream:
          Type: Kinesis
          Properties:
            # If the stream is in the same account, just put the stream name e.g,
            # Stream: kinesis-dso-test-stream
            Stream: arn:aws:kinesis:eu-west-1:565687767411:stream/kinesis-dso-test-stream

  LambdaEventPusher:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Tags:
        application: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
        created_by: cloudformation
        owner: <my-team>
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: app.handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      MemorySize: 128
      Timeout: 6
      Environment:
        Variables:
          LOG_LEVEL: debug

Outputs:
  IamRoleArn:
    Description: "IAM Role ARN attached to function"
    Value: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
```

</details>

79	  RealtimeProductRecommendationsVersion:
80	    Type: AWS::Lambda::Version
81	    DependsOn: RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
82	    Properties:
83	      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
84	      Description: !Sub "deploy-${Environment}-${ImageTag}"
85	
86	  # New alias per deploy (lets us fully warm the new alias before switching ALB over)
87	  RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias:
88	    Type: AWS::Lambda::Alias
89	    Properties:
90	      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
91	      FunctionVersion: !GetAtt RealtimeProductRecommendationsVersion.Version
92	      Name: !Sub "live-${ImageTag}"
93	      ProvisionedConcurrencyConfig:
94	        ProvisionedConcurrentExecutions: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyDefault
95	
96	  ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget:
97	    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalableTarget
98	    DependsOn:
99	      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
100	    Properties:
101	      ServiceNamespace: lambda
102	      ScalableDimension: lambda:function:ProvisionedConcurrency
103	      ResourceId: !Sub "function:${RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction}:live-${ImageTag}"
104	      MinCapacity: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum
105	      MaxCapacity: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMaximum
106	      RoleARN: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/aws-service-role/lambda.application-autoscaling.amazonaws.com/AWSServiceRoleForApplicationAutoScaling_LambdaConcurrency"
107	
108	  ProvisionedConcurrencyTargetTracking:
109	    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalingPolicy
110	    DependsOn:
111	      - ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget
112	    Properties:
113	      PolicyName: ProvisionedConcurrencyTargetTracking
114	      PolicyType: TargetTrackingScaling
115	      ScalingTargetId: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget
116	      TargetTrackingScalingPolicyConfiguration:
117	        PredefinedMetricSpecification:
118	          PredefinedMetricType: LambdaProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization
119	        TargetValue: !Ref TargetUtilization
120	        ScaleInCooldown: 180  # If we scale down, wait 3 minutes before trying to scale down again
121	        ScaleOutCooldown: 60  # If we scale up, give provisioned concurrency time to reprovision and then 8 seconds for our lambda to initialize
122	
123	  # CloudWatch alarm: fires ONLY when metric is missing. This is used to scale us down when there is absolutely no activity, such as in dev environments.
124	  # This is needed as the scaling policy only triggers when there is activity on the lambda: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/provisioned-concurrency.html
125	  PcNoDataAlarm:
126	    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
127	    DependsOn:
128	      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
129	    Properties:
130	      AlarmName: !Sub "data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-PcNoData-${Environment}"
131	      AlarmDescription: "No ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization datapoints => scale provisioned concurrency to minimum"
132	      Namespace: AWS/Lambda
133	      MetricName: ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization
134	      Dimensions:
135	        - Name: FunctionName
136	          Value: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
137	        - Name: Resource
138	          Value: !Sub "${RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction}:live-${ImageTag}"
139	      Statistic: Maximum
140	      Period: 120
141	      EvaluationPeriods: 1  # Trigger on 'no data' for 2 minutes. But use 1 period, so as to reset the alarm after any activity, so it can re-trigger.
142	      ComparisonOperator: LessThanThreshold
143	      Threshold: -1             # impossible value => only 'missing' can trigger the alarm
144	      TreatMissingData: missing # missing datapoints => INSUFFICIENT_DATA
145	      # Trigger Lambda specifically when alarm enters "no data" / INSUFFICIENT_DATA
146	      InsufficientDataActions:
147	        - !GetAtt ScaleToMinFunction.Arn
148	
149	  AllowAlarmInvokeScaleToMin:
150	    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
151	    Properties:
152	      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
153	      FunctionName: !Ref ScaleToMinFunction
154	      Principal: lambda.alarms.cloudwatch.amazonaws.com
155	      SourceArn: !GetAtt PcNoDataAlarm.Arn
156	
157	  ScaleToMinFunction:
158	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
159	    Properties:
160	      FunctionName: !Sub "data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-scale-to-min-${Environment}"
161	      Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/realtime-recommendations-scale-to-zero"
162	      Runtime: python3.13
163	      Handler: index.handler
164	      Timeout: 30
165	      MemorySize: 128
166	      Environment:
167	        Variables:
168	          TARGET_FUNCTION_NAME: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
169	          TARGET_ALIAS: !Sub "live-${ImageTag}"
170	          MIN_PC: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum
171	      InlineCode: |
172	        import os, boto3
173	        lambda_client = boto3.client("lambda")
174	
175	        def handler(event, context):
176	          fn = os.environ["TARGET_FUNCTION_NAME"]
177	          alias = os.environ["TARGET_ALIAS"]
178	          min_pc = int(os.environ["MIN_PC"])
179	          # Set provisioned concurrency on alias to minimum
180	          lambda_client.put_provisioned_concurrency_config(
181	            FunctionName=fn,
182	            Qualifier=alias,
183	            ProvisionedConcurrentExecutions=min_pc,
184	          )
185	          return {"ok": True, "function": fn, "alias": alias, "min_pc": min_pc}
186	
187	  # Allow ALB to invoke the (new) Lambda alias
188	  LoadBalancerLambdaPermission:
189	    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
190	    DependsOn:
191	      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
192	    Properties:
193	      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
194	      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
195	      Principal: elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com
196	
197	  ServiceListenerTargetGroup:
198	    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup
199	    DependsOn:
200	      - LoadBalancerLambdaPermission
201	      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias  # don’t switch until ProvisionedConcurrency is ready, to prevent downtime during deployments
202	    Properties:
203	      Name: !Sub "realtime-product-recs-${Environment}"
204	      TargetType: lambda
205	      Targets:
206	        - Id: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
207	
208	  ServiceListenerRule:
209	    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::ListenerRule
210	    Properties:
211	      Actions:
212	        - Type: forward
213	          TargetGroupArn: !Ref ServiceListenerTargetGroup
214	      Conditions:
215	        - Field: http-request-method
216	          HttpRequestMethodConfig:
217	            Values: ['POST']
218	        - Field: path-pattern
219	          PathPatternConfig:
220	            Values: ['/realtime_product_recommendations']
221	      ListenerArn: !Ref ListenerRuleArn
222	      Priority: !Ref ListenerRulePriority
223	
224	#  # Share cross-account lambda invocation permissions to the dev and prod accounts
225	#  DevLambdaPermission:

file.content:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: Realtime Product Recommendations API

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  ImageTag:
    Description: "Image SHA"
    Type: String
  LogLevel:
    Description: Log level can be DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR or CRITICAL
    Type: String
    Default: 'INFO'
  DynamoDBTable:
    Description: Name of the DynamoDB table to read affinity-events from
    Type: String
  NumberOfSkusToReturn:
    Description: Number of SKUs that should be returned as product recommendations
    Type: Number
    Default: 10
  NumberOfEventsToQuery:
    Description: Max number of affinity-events that should be queried from the DynamoDB table per session ID. Prevents slow performance or out-of-memory crash.
    Type: Number
    Default: 10
  ListenerRuleArn:
    Description: ARN of the Load-balancer-listener-rule that the realtime-product-recommendations API will be exposed behind
    Type: String
  ListenerRulePriority:
    Description: Load balancer rule priority, used so that different dev versions can coexist in the same load balancer by using different listener rule priorities.
    Type: Number
  ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum:
    Type: Number
    Default: 1  # Minimum provisioned concurrency cost of 1x 1769MB lambda instance = $20.80 / month
  ProvisionedConcurrencyMaximum:
    Type: Number
    Default: 10  # In case these are left running at max, the cost estimate of running 10x 1769MB lambda instances = $208 / month
  ProvisionedConcurrencyDefault:
    Type: Number
    Default: 1
  TargetUtilization:
    Description: Provisioned concurrency is autoscaled to target this usage ratio of maximum-concurrent-requests / provisioned-concurrency.
    Type: Number
    Default: 0.20


Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-${Environment}'
      Targets:
        DynamoDB:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Ref DynamoDBTable

  RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          Env: !Ref Environment
          DynamoDBTable: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          NumberOfSkusToReturn: !Ref NumberOfSkusToReturn
          NumberOfEventsToQuery: !Ref NumberOfEventsToQuery
          LogLevel: !Ref LogLevel
      PackageType: Image
      ImageUri: !Sub "334512769709.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/data-de/realtime-product-recommendations-api/${Environment}:${ImageTag}"
      Architectures: [x86_64]
      MemorySize: 1769  # Minimum memory for which we still get 1 full vCPU
      Timeout: 60

  # Publish an immutable version every deploy (Description changes => new Version resource)
  RealtimeProductRecommendationsVersion:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Version
    DependsOn: RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
      Description: !Sub "deploy-${Environment}-${ImageTag}"

  # New alias per deploy (lets us fully warm the new alias before switching ALB over)
  RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Alias
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
      FunctionVersion: !GetAtt RealtimeProductRecommendationsVersion.Version
      Name: !Sub "live-${ImageTag}"
      ProvisionedConcurrencyConfig:
        ProvisionedConcurrentExecutions: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyDefault

  ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget:
    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalableTarget
    DependsOn:
      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
    Properties:
      ServiceNamespace: lambda
      ScalableDimension: lambda:function:ProvisionedConcurrency
      ResourceId: !Sub "function:${RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction}:live-${ImageTag}"
      MinCapacity: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum
      MaxCapacity: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMaximum
      RoleARN: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/aws-service-role/lambda.application-autoscaling.amazonaws.com/AWSServiceRoleForApplicationAutoScaling_LambdaConcurrency"

  ProvisionedConcurrencyTargetTracking:
    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalingPolicy
    DependsOn:
      - ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget
    Properties:
      PolicyName: ProvisionedConcurrencyTargetTracking
      PolicyType: TargetTrackingScaling
      ScalingTargetId: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget
      TargetTrackingScalingPolicyConfiguration:
        PredefinedMetricSpecification:
          PredefinedMetricType: LambdaProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization
        TargetValue: !Ref TargetUtilization
        ScaleInCooldown: 180  # If we scale down, wait 3 minutes before trying to scale down again
        ScaleOutCooldown: 60  # If we scale up, give provisioned concurrency time to reprovision and then 8 seconds for our lambda to initialize

  # CloudWatch alarm: fires ONLY when metric is missing. This is used to scale us down when there is absolutely no activity, such as in dev environments.
  # This is needed as the scaling policy only triggers when there is activity on the lambda: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/provisioned-concurrency.html
  PcNoDataAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    DependsOn:
      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub "data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-PcNoData-${Environment}"
      AlarmDescription: "No ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization datapoints => scale provisioned concurrency to minimum"
      Namespace: AWS/Lambda
      MetricName: ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization
      Dimensions:
        - Name: FunctionName
          Value: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
        - Name: Resource
          Value: !Sub "${RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction}:live-${ImageTag}"
      Statistic: Maximum
      Period: 120
      EvaluationPeriods: 1  # Trigger on 'no data' for 2 minutes. But use 1 period, so as to reset the alarm after any activity, so it can re-trigger.
      ComparisonOperator: LessThanThreshold
      Threshold: -1             # impossible value => only 'missing' can trigger the alarm
      TreatMissingData: missing # missing datapoints => INSUFFICIENT_DATA
      # Trigger Lambda specifically when alarm enters "no data" / INSUFFICIENT_DATA
      InsufficientDataActions:
        - !GetAtt ScaleToMinFunction.Arn

  AllowAlarmInvokeScaleToMin:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      FunctionName: !Ref ScaleToMinFunction
      Principal: lambda.alarms.cloudwatch.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !GetAtt PcNoDataAlarm.Arn

  ScaleToMinFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-scale-to-min-${Environment}"
      Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/realtime-recommendations-scale-to-zero"
      Runtime: python3.13
      Handler: index.handler
      Timeout: 30
      MemorySize: 128
      Environment:
        Variables:
          TARGET_FUNCTION_NAME: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
          TARGET_ALIAS: !Sub "live-${ImageTag}"
          MIN_PC: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum
      InlineCode: |
        import os, boto3
        lambda_client = boto3.client("lambda")

        def handler(event, context):
          fn = os.environ["TARGET_FUNCTION_NAME"]
          alias = os.environ["TARGET_ALIAS"]
          min_pc = int(os.environ["MIN_PC"])
          # Set provisioned concurrency on alias to minimum
          lambda_client.put_provisioned_concurrency_config(
            FunctionName=fn,
            Qualifier=alias,
            ProvisionedConcurrentExecutions=min_pc,
          )
          return {"ok": True, "function": fn, "alias": alias, "min_pc": min_pc}

  # Allow ALB to invoke the (new) Lambda alias
  LoadBalancerLambdaPermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    DependsOn:
      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
    Properties:
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
      Principal: elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com

  ServiceListenerTargetGroup:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup
    DependsOn:
      - LoadBalancerLambdaPermission
      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias  # don’t switch until ProvisionedConcurrency is ready, to prevent downtime during deployments
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "realtime-product-recs-${Environment}"
      TargetType: lambda
      Targets:
        - Id: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias

  ServiceListenerRule:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::ListenerRule
    Properties:
      Actions:
        - Type: forward
          TargetGroupArn: !Ref ServiceListenerTargetGroup
      Conditions:
        - Field: http-request-method
          HttpRequestMethodConfig:
            Values: ['POST']
        - Field: path-pattern
          PathPatternConfig:
            Values: ['/realtime_product_recommendations']
      ListenerArn: !Ref ListenerRuleArn
      Priority: !Ref ListenerRulePriority

#  # Share cross-account lambda invocation permissions to the dev and prod accounts
#  DevLambdaPermission:
#    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
#    Properties:
#      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
#      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Alias
#      Principal: "arn:aws:iam::448714108511:root"   # dev account
#
#  ProdLambdaPermission:
#    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
#    Properties:
#      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
#      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Alias
#      Principal: "arn:aws:iam::482520917892:root"   # prod account

Outputs:
  RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunctionARN:
    Description: "Realtime product recommendations API lambda ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Arn
